title: Libertas - Freedom Tech Signals
description: Curated insights on freedom technology: Bitcoin, privacy tools, censorship resistance, and digital sovereignty.

items.title: CBP Signs Deal with Clearview AI for Border Surveillance Face Recognition
items.summary: US Border Patrol intelligence units will deploy Clearview AI's facial recognition system built on billions of scraped internet images for 'tactical targeting' operations.
items.content_text:
US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) signed contract with Clearview AI for facial recognition access
• System built on billions of images scraped from social media and internet without consent
• Border Patrol intelligence units will use tool for 'tactical targeting' operations
• Expands government surveillance capabilities at borders and potentially inland
• Threatens anonymity of travelers, activists, immigrants, and anyone with online presence
• Clearview AI previously faced lawsuits and bans over privacy violations
• Deal represents normalization of mass biometric surveillance infrastructure

items.title: DHS Plans Unified Biometric Platform After Dismantling Privacy Reviews
items.summary: Department of Homeland Security seeks to merge face and fingerprint databases into single platform while removing centralized privacy oversight and facial recognition limits.
items.content_text:
DHS planning to consolidate separate biometric systems into unified search platform
• Move follows dismantling of centralized privacy reviews within the department
• Key limitations on facial recognition technology have been removed
• Consolidation would enable cross-agency biometric data sharing at unprecedented scale
• Privacy safeguards eliminated before expanding surveillance capabilities
• Platform would allow simultaneous face and fingerprint searches across agencies
• Represents significant expansion of federal biometric surveillance infrastructure
• Timing suggests deliberate weakening of oversight before capability enhancement

items.title: EFF Calls on Tech Giants to Resist DHS's Unlawful Surveillance Subpoenas Targeting Activists
items.summary: EFF and ACLU are demanding major tech companies require court orders before complying with DHS subpoenas that target First Amendment activities, after DHS withdrew challenged subpoenas rather than defend them in court.
items.content_text:
EFF sent open letter to 10 major tech companies (Meta, Google, Amazon, Apple, Discord, Microsoft, Reddit, SNAP, TikTok, X) demanding stronger resistance to DHS subpoenas
• DHS has systematically targeted users documenting ICE activities, criticizing government, and attending protests using administrative subpoenas
• When challenged in court, DHS withdrew subpoenas rather than defend their legality, proving government knows they're unlawful
• Administrative subpoenas don't require judicial approval, unlike search warrants - companies can legally refuse without court order
• Google complied with subpoena targeting Cornell PhD student who attended Gaza protest, breaking their own user notification promise
• DHS subpoenaed Meta to unmask anonymous Instagram accounts tracking ICE raids in California and Pennsylvania
• EFF demands: require court intervention, maximize user notification, resist gag orders that prevent user notification

items.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalists' Equipment, Threaten Sources Over US Deportation Story
items.summary: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists for 5 hours, seized phones/laptops, and threatened to 'crush' them while investigating secret US migrant deportations, raising source protection concerns.
items.content_text:
Four journalists detained by Cameroon judicial police while reporting on secret US deportations
• Police seized cellphones, laptop, and other equipment - devices not yet returned
• AP reporters Nalova Akua, Angel Ngwe, Arnold Ndal plus freelancer Randy Joe Sa'ah affected
• Journalists threatened they would be 'crushed' and would 'disappear without a trace'
• Police investigating 'criminal trespass' charges for reporting at deportation center
• Lawyer concerned police will circumvent device passwords and compromise anonymous sources
• Story covered secret deportation flights of African migrants under Trump administration
• Demonstrates operational security risks for journalists in hostile environments

items.title: Citizen Lab Reports on Transnational Repression Methods to EU Parliament
items.summary: Citizen Lab researchers present findings on transnational repression tactics within EU to Parliament committee, recommending EU-wide TNR definition and central knowledge hub for counter-strategies.
items.content_text:
Citizen Lab presenting transnational repression (TNR) report to EU Parliament Foreign Affairs Committee on January 28
• Research team includes senior associate Emile Dirks and independent researchers examining TNR within EU borders
• Report analyzes perpetrators and methods of cross-border surveillance and harassment targeting activists
• Key recommendations include developing EU-wide TNR definition and creating central 'knowledge hub'
• Study addresses systematic methods used to intimidate civil society and digital rights advocates
• EU Parliament setting suggests potential policy responses to protect activists from state-sponsored harassment
• Citizen Lab's involvement brings technical expertise on digital surveillance methods to policy discussions

items.title: Pentagon Threatens AI Company Anthropic Over Surveillance Restrictions
items.summary: Department of Defense threatening to label Anthropic a "supply chain risk" after company refused to lift restrictions on surveillance and autonomous weapons use of their AI.
items.content_text:
Pentagon gave ultimatum to Anthropic demanding removal of restrictions on military AI use
• Anthropic maintains "bright red lines" against surveillance of US persons and autonomous weapons
• DoD threatens "supply chain risk" label that would ban other defense contractors from using Anthropic's AI
• Dispute began after Anthropic suspected their AI was used in January 3, 2026 attack on Venezuela via Palantir partnership
• Anthropic was first AI company cleared for classified operations in 2025
• EFF urges Anthropic to resist government pressure and maintain ethical restrictions
• Case represents broader pattern of government pressure on tech companies to enable surveillance

items.title: UN Rules Pakistani Human Rights Defender's Detention Violates International Law
items.summary: UN Working Group found Pakistan's imprisonment of Pashtun human rights defender Idris Khattak unlawful, calling for immediate release after 5+ years detention for documenting enforced disappearances.
items.content_text:
UN Working Group on Arbitrary Detention ruled Idris Khattak's detention violates international law
• Khattak is Pashtun human rights defender known as 'Pakistan's expert on enforced disappearances'
• Abducted November 2019, disappeared for 7 months, then convicted by military court under Official Secrets Act
• WGAD found he was detained specifically for his human rights work and freedom of expression
• Due process violations included: 2 years pretrial detention, denied counsel, forced confession, secret evidence
• Military court conviction improper under international law for civilian human rights defender
• Pakistan's Freedom House score declined from 43/100 (2017) to 32/100 (2025)
• Case represents broader pattern of silencing dissent and targeting minority rights defenders

items.title: New Attacks on Fiat-Shamir Zero-Knowledge Proofs Threaten Privacy Systems
items.summary: Researchers found practical attacks against Fiat-Shamir-based zero-knowledge proof systems, potentially compromising privacy protocols that rely on these cryptographic foundations.
items.content_text:
Khovratovich, Rothblum and Soukhanov published 'How to Prove False Statements' showing attacks on Fiat-Shamir
• Targets GKR15 proof system and similar interactive protocols converted to non-interactive using Fiat-Shamir heuristic
• Attack exploits 'weak challenges' - specific challenge values that allow proving false statements
• Affects proof systems used in privacy-preserving applications and blockchain protocols
• Interactive versions remain secure, but non-interactive Fiat-Shamir conversions vulnerable
• Hash functions like SHA-3 replacing random oracles create exploitable gaps in security
• Threatens zero-knowledge systems used for private transactions and anonymous credentials

items.title: Signal Launches Built-in Censorship Circumvention with TLS Proxy Support
items.summary: Signal deployed built-in censorship circumvention features including TLS proxy support to help users bypass government blocks and maintain private communications.
items.content_text:
Signal officially launched built-in censorship circumvention functionality
• Feature includes support for simple TLS proxy to bypass government blocks
• Response to recent Signal blocks in multiple countries
• Designed to help users maintain trusted, safe communication channels
• TLS proxy can circumvent blocks "in many circumstances" according to Signal
• Feature is now integrated directly into Signal app, no external tools needed
• Represents Signal's proactive approach to anti-censorship infrastructure

items.title: OONI Documents Media Censorship During India-Pakistan Conflict
items.summary: OONI and Digital Rights Foundation documented coordinated media censorship by both India and Pakistan during their May 2025 conflict, with governments blocking news outlets citing 'disinformation' and 'hate speech.'
items.content_text:
4-day India-Pakistan conflict in May 2025 triggered coordinated media censorship
• Both governments blocked multiple news media outlets during the armed conflict
• Censorship justified as fighting 'disinformation campaigns' and 'hate speech'
• OONI (Open Observatory of Network Interference) measured and documented the blocks
• Collaboration with Digital Rights Foundation Pakistan provided on-ground context
• Report provides technical evidence of information control tactics during crisis
• Demonstrates how governments use conflict as pretext for expanded censorship
• OONI's measurement infrastructure captured real-time censorship deployment

items.title: Signal Announces New Features Roll Call Blog Series for App Improvements
items.summary: Signal launched a periodic blog series to highlight new features and quality of life improvements, emphasizing their commitment to truly private messaging without data harvesting for ads or AI training.
items.content_text:
Signal announced new 'New Features Roll Call' periodic blog series
• Series will highlight multiple features and improvements in single posts
• Signal reaffirms commitment to private messaging without data harvesting
• Emphasizes protection against targeted advertising and AI training on user data
• Focus on improving user experience while maintaining privacy guarantees
• Represents ongoing effort to communicate development progress to users
• Reinforces Signal's position as privacy-first alternative to mainstream platforms

items.title: India Civil Society Updates OONI Website Censorship Test Lists
items.summary: The Bachchao Project and OONI collaborated to update the website list used for monitoring internet censorship in India, strengthening civil society's ability to track government blocking.
items.content_text:
The Bachchao Project partnered with OONI to review India's website test list in June 2025
• Updated list used by OONI Probe to monitor website censorship across India
• Workshop involved experts updating specific categories of tested websites
• Part of broader effort to strengthen civil society engagement in internet freedom monitoring
• Test list maintained by Citizen Lab contains URLs regularly tested for blocking
• Enhanced monitoring capabilities provide better visibility into government censorship patterns
• Represents organized approach to documenting digital rights violations in India

items.title: Federal Agents Clone Marine Veteran's Phone Without Warrant After Minneapolis Shooting
items.summary: Federal ICE/Border Patrol agents detained Marine veteran Steven Saari for 6+ hours, forced biometric collection, and cloned his phone without a warrant after he observed the Alex Pretti shooting scene in Minneapolis.
items.content_text:
Steven Saari, Iraq/Afghanistan Marine veteran, observed federal shooting scene in Minneapolis
• Federal agents initially mistook him for undercover officer, then detained him for 6+ hours
• Agents forced biometric data collection including facial scans and DNA samples
• Phone was cloned without warrant, consent, or judicial authorization according to Saari
• Detention included denial of attorney access despite repeated requests
• Legal experts cite clear Fourth Amendment violations referencing Riley v. California (2014)
• ACLU notes narrow emergency exceptions don't apply once person and device are secured
• Released without charges into sub-zero weather with no transportation

items.title: FCC Chairman Urges Broadcasters to Join 'Pledge America Campaign' for Pro-America Programming
items.summary: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr launched a campaign asking broadcasters to voluntarily air patriotic programming including daily Pledge of Allegiance and civic education content for Trump's America 250th celebration.
items.content_text:
FCC Chairman Brendan Carr established 'Pledge America Campaign' tied to Trump's 'Salute to America 250' project
• Campaign asks broadcasters to voluntarily pledge to air 'patriotic, pro-America programming'
• Suggested content includes daily Pledge of Allegiance, Star Spangled Banner, civic education PSAs
• Examples include American history segments, patriotic music, and National Park Service site features
• Framed as voluntary and 'non-partisan' despite explicit political ties
• Represents potential soft editorial pressure from federal communications regulator
• Raises concerns about government influence over media content decisions

items.title: Senegal Implements Multi-Phase Internet Shutdowns During Political Protests
items.summary: NetBlocks documented systematic internet censorship in Senegal during 2023 protests, including social media blocks, mobile data curfews, and platform-specific restrictions targeting opposition to Ousmane Sonko's sentencing.
items.content_text:
Social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, WhatsApp, Instagram, YouTube, Telegram) restricted starting June 1, 2023
• Mobile data completely suspended June 4-7 in daily curfew-style pattern (1pm-2am local time)
• Restrictions triggered by protests over opposition leader Ousmane Sonko's sentencing and arrest
• Second wave of mobile data curfews began July 31 after Sonko's arrest, lasting at least 6 days
• TikTok specifically blocked August 2 due to alleged 'hateful and subversive messages'
• Shutdowns affected major providers Orange (Sonatel) and Free (Tigo) based on 4000 measurements
• VPNs could circumvent social media blocks but not mobile data shutdowns
• Government also faced activist cyberattacks that took down state websites on ADIE network

items.title: Signal Rolls Out Secure Backups Feature After Years of User Requests
items.summary: Signal launches opt-in secure backups in Android beta, addressing the top user request to preserve encrypted message history when phones are lost or damaged.
items.content_text:
Signal begins rolling out secure backups feature after extensive development
• Feature is opt-in and currently available in Android beta version only
• Addresses most common user request for message history preservation
• Enables recovery of messages, media, and documents if phone is lost/damaged
• iOS and Desktop versions planned for near future release
• Represents significant UX improvement for encrypted communications adoption
• Maintains Signal's security model while solving usability gap

items.title: Iran Conducts Mass Arrests, Enforced Disappearances Following January 2026 Massacre
items.summary: Iran arrested tens of thousands following January 8-9 massacres, conducting mass enforced disappearances, torture, and death sentences in coordinated crackdown to suppress dissent.
items.content_text:
Tens of thousands arrested since December 28, 2025 following countrywide protests and January 8-9, 2026 massacres
• IRGC Intelligence Organization summoned at least 11,000 people as of January 26, with 8,843 indictments issued by February 17
• Authorities conducting enforced disappearances by refusing to disclose detainees' whereabouts or fate
• Systematic torture documented including beatings, sexual violence, food deprivation, and psychological torture
• Death sentences issued including 19-year-old Saleh Mohammadi sentenced to public execution after forced confession under torture
• Heavy military presence and martial law-like restrictions imposed across multiple cities
• Over 2,800 named detainees documented by activist networks, with state broadcasting forced confessions including children
• Pattern mirrors 2022 crackdown tactics that resulted in arbitrary executions

items.title: Zimbabwe Detains Journalist for Week Over Online Corruption Report
items.summary: Zimbabwean authorities detained Bulawayo24 journalist Gideon Madzikatidze for a week on cyberbullying and broadcasting charges after he published online corruption allegations against waste management company.
items.content_text:
Journalist Gideon Madzikatidze detained since February 18 on cyberbullying and broadcasting without license charges
• Bulawayo24 reporter published story alleging Geo Pomona Waste Management bribed journalists to avoid scrutiny
• Faces up to 10 years prison for cyberbullying under Criminal Law Act, 3 months under Broadcasting Services Act
• Authorities charging him under broadcast licensing law despite story only appearing on website
• Committee to Protect Journalists calls detention 'totally disproportionate response' to legitimate reporting
• Case demonstrates how cybercrime and licensing laws criminalize online journalism in Zimbabwe
• Pattern shows regulatory capture of internet publishing under traditional broadcast licensing framework

items.title: 17 Orgs Fight FTC's Retaliatory Investigation Into Media Matters
items.summary: Coalition led by The Intercept filed amicus brief to stop FTC's allegedly retaliatory investigation into Media Matters after they published critical Trump administration coverage.
items.content_text:
17 nonprofits filed amicus brief urging D.C. Circuit Court to halt FTC investigation into Media Matters
• Investigation allegedly launched after Media Matters published critical reporting on Trump allies
• Brief describes 'pretextual investigations' as troublingly common government intimidation tactic
• Coalition includes EFF, Freedom of the Press Foundation, Center for Investigative Reporting
• Pattern cited across agencies: DOJ, FCC using similar coercive investigation tactics
• Brief seeks to uphold preliminary injunction against the investigation
• Argues such investigations chill coverage adverse to those in power
• Mentions related press freedom attacks: journalist arrests in Minnesota, seizure of communications in D.C.

items.title: Senator Blumenthal Opens Binance Investigation Over Iran Sanctions Evasion Reports
items.summary: US Senator Richard Blumenthal launched an inquiry into Binance following reports the exchange may have facilitated Iran-linked sanctions violations.
items.content_text:
Senator Richard Blumenthal opened formal inquiry into Binance
• Investigation triggered by reports of potential Iran-linked sanctions violations
• Follows pattern of crypto exchanges being scrutinized for sanctions compliance
• Iran has been subject to extensive US economic sanctions for decades
• Highlights ongoing tension between financial sovereignty tools and state controls
• Could signal increased regulatory pressure on major crypto exchanges
• Demonstrates real-world use of crypto for circumventing economic restrictions

items.title: Signal Announces New President Role to Focus on Sustainability and Strategy
items.summary: Signal appointed a new President on September 12 to focus on long-term sustainability, strategy, and communications for the encrypted messaging platform.
items.content_text:
Signal created new President position filled by longtime board member
• Role focuses on financial sustainability and strategic planning
• President will work on broadening Signal's public communications
• Appointee has been involved with Signal since RedPhone/TextSecure era
• Signal emphasizes importance of building 'long taproot' for future resilience
• Organizational restructuring aimed at strengthening both app and organization
• Move signals Signal's focus on long-term institutional stability

items.title: Ukrainian Authorities Investigated for Surveilling 10 Anti-Corruption Journalists
items.summary: Ukrainian police opened criminal investigation after NABU uncovered alleged surveillance of 10 journalists covering corruption, with their personal data found on seized devices from a criminal organization involving government officials.
items.content_text:
National Anti-Corruption Bureau of Ukraine (NABU) discovered personal information of 10 investigative journalists on seized digital devices during 15-month 'Operation Midas'
• Criminal organization allegedly included government officials and businessmen who embezzled energy sector funds and collected intelligence on journalists, politicians, and anti-corruption officials
• Ukrainian national police launched criminal investigation to determine if journalists' privacy was violated
• Nine journalists identified include editors from major outlets like Forbes Ukraine, Censor.NET, and investigative platforms Nashi Groshi and Ukrainska Pravda
• Follows pattern of surveillance targeting Ukrainian investigative journalists - in 2024, SBU's Department for Protection of National Statehood spied on 30 journalists at Bihus.Info
• Committee to Protect Journalists calls for accountability, noting surveillance threatens corruption investigations
• Case highlights state-level surveillance infrastructure targeting press freedom advocates

items.title: Israel Blocks 37 Aid Groups Over Staff Data Requirements, Citing Security
items.summary: Israeli authorities are barring 37 international NGOs from Gaza/West Bank operations after they refused new registration requirements demanding staff lists and biodata, citing violations of humanitarian neutrality principles.
items.content_text:
37 international NGOs face operational ban starting March 1, 2026 for refusing new Israeli registration requirements
• Government Resolution No. 2542 mandates organizations provide complete staff lists and personal biodata to Ministry of Diaspora Affairs
• Aid groups including Oxfam, MSF, Save the Children refuse citing humanitarian neutrality principles and data protection obligations
• Organizations argue requirements create surveillance mechanism and compromise staff safety in conflict zone
• Ban affects groups running 60% of field hospitals and all child malnutrition centers in Gaza
• Law gives Israeli government broad power to deny registration for 'public safety or state security' risks
• Only 27 organizations have completed approved registration under new requirements
• UN estimates closure would immediately shut one-third of remaining health facilities in Gaza

items.title: UK Issues Secret Order for Apple iCloud Encryption Backdoor, Global Impact Feared
items.summary: UK government secretly ordered Apple to backdoor end-to-end encrypted iCloud backups globally. Apple disabled Advanced Data Protection for UK users rather than comply, highlighting encryption policy precedent risks.
items.content_text:
UK issued secret Technical Capability Notice demanding backdoor access to Apple's end-to-end encrypted iCloud backups [Washington Post]
• Order requires 'blanket capability to view fully encrypted material' globally, not just UK users
• Apple responded by disabling Advanced Data Protection feature for all UK users rather than comply
• UK's 2016 Investigatory Powers Act enables secret demands that don't distinguish between UK and non-UK customers
• Apple's lawyers warned in 2024 that UK could become 'arbiter of all cybersecurity defense measures globally'
• Technical implementation would likely require global backdoor due to operational complexity
• Sets dangerous precedent where single nation could dictate encryption standards worldwide
• Compliance could trigger similar demands from China, US, and other governments

items.title: FBI Asset Helped Run Dark Web Market for Years, Approved Fentanyl Sales
items.summary: FBI secretly controlled a staffer at Incognito dark web market who approved sales of fentanyl-laced drugs, revealing extent of law enforcement infiltration of anonymous marketplaces.
items.content_text:
FBI asset was embedded as staffer within Incognito dark web marketplace operations
• Asset reportedly approved sales of fentanyl-tainted pills over extended period
• Sales included drugs from dealer linked to confirmed overdose death
• Reveals FBI's willingness to allow harmful drug sales to maintain operational cover
• Demonstrates sophisticated law enforcement infiltration of Tor-based markets
• Raises questions about liability for deaths occurring during FBI operations
• Shows dark web market operators face threats from both exit scams and law enforcement control

items.title: Pakistan Implements Total Internet Shutdowns During Political Arrests
items.summary: Pakistan disrupted social media platforms and imposed total mobile internet shutdowns following former PM Imran Khan's arrest, with NetBlocks confirming multi-day blackouts affecting fundamental rights.
items.content_text:
NetBlocks confirmed disruption of Twitter, Facebook, and YouTube across multiple Pakistani ISPs on May 9, 2023
• Total internet shutdowns implemented on mobile networks in some regions during former PM Imran Khan's arrest
• Social media restrictions could be circumvented with VPNs, but mobile broadband shutdowns created total blackouts
• Disruptions continued for multiple days, intensifying in some areas with Instagram also becoming unreachable
• Hundreds of protesters reportedly detained during the information blackout period
• NetBlocks used 120 measurements from 60 vantage points across Pakistan to document the censorship
• Shutdowns demonstrate government willingness to use internet disruption as tool for political control

items.title: Russia's Media Crackdown: 27 Jailed, 355 Labeled 'Foreign Agents', 25K+ Sites Blocked
items.summary: CPJ documents unprecedented Russian media repression since 2022: 27 journalists imprisoned, 355 outlets labeled 'foreign agents', and over 25,000 websites blocked, creating urgent need for censorship-resistant tools.
items.content_text:
27 journalists imprisoned on criminal charges since Feb 2022 (15 Russian, 10 Ukrainian, 2 American)
• 355 journalists and media outlets branded 'foreign agents' requiring detailed government reporting
• 27 exiled journalists sentenced to jail in absentia for continued reporting
• Over 25,000 websites blocked in connection with war reporting coverage
• 9 journalists jailed for 'fake' news, 14 for terrorism/extremism charges
• Recent cases include detention of journalists reporting on protests and opposition activities
• New laws allow prosecution of 'foreign agents' in absentia for violations committed abroad
• Pattern shows escalating repression making secure communication tools critical for journalists

items.title: EFF Defends Section 230 as Essential Protection for Online Speech and Freedom Tech
items.summary: EFF argues Section 230's immunity protections are critical for free expression online, warning that weakening them would force platforms to pre-censor content and chill speech across the internet.
items.content_text:
EFF published op-ed defending Section 230 on its 30th anniversary
• Section 230 prevents civil suits against online services based on user-generated content
• Law protects both platforms and users who act as intermediaries (forwarding emails, retweeting)
• Alternatives like duty-of-care standards would create chilling effects and mass takedowns
• Notice-and-takedown systems invite abuse and lead to over-censorship
• AI cannot make legal determinations about content at scale
• Without immunity, platforms would need to review all content before publication
• Benefits small platforms and users, not just 'big tech' companies
• Essential for email, messaging, blogging, and social media to function

items.title: EFF Hosts Livestream on Fighting Flock ALPR Surveillance in Cities
items.summary: EFF is hosting a livestream Feb 19 on fighting Flock automated license plate readers, featuring case studies of successful campaigns that blocked surveillance deployments across US cities.
items.content_text:
EFF hosting 'Get the Flock Out of Our City' livestream February 19, 12-1PM Pacific
• Event focuses on fighting Flock Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) surveillance systems
• Speakers include EFF staff, Fight for the Future, and Rural Privacy Coalition representatives
• Will cover legal implications of ALPR data collection and successful resistance campaigns
• Cities from Austin to Cambridge to Texas towns have rejected Flock contracts
• Evidence shows Flock systems used to surveil abortion seekers, protesters, and targeted communities
• Free event includes live Q&A and will be recorded for later viewing
• Demonstrates growing organized resistance to municipal surveillance infrastructure

items.title: Amazon Ring Expands Biometric Surveillance Network with AI-Powered Pet Tracking
items.summary: Amazon Ring's new 'Search Party' feature uses AI biometrics to scan neighborhood camera networks, expanding surveillance infrastructure with law enforcement partnerships despite privacy violations.
items.content_text:
Ring launched 'Search Party' feature that uses AI to scan footage across neighborhood Ring devices for biometric identification
• Feature builds on existing 'Familiar Faces' face recognition technology, creating potential for combined neighborhood-wide facial surveillance
• Search Party is enabled by default, requiring users to manually opt-out through app settings
• Ring maintains partnerships with police surveillance companies Axon and Flock Safety after claiming to end warrantless access in 2024
• EFF warns this creates infrastructure for pervasive street-level surveillance disguised as pet recovery
• Ring has history of FTC violations including employee access to customer footage and warrantless police data sharing
• Feature may violate biometric privacy laws in states requiring explicit consent for facial recognition

items.title: Thai Court Sentences 4 Free Speech Activists to 32 Months for Royal Criticism
items.summary: Thai activists including Pimsiri Petchnamrob received 32-month prison sentences for criticizing the monarchy under lese majeste laws, highlighting severe censorship repression in Thailand.
items.content_text:
Bangkok Criminal Court sentenced 4 democracy activists to 32 months prison for lese majeste violations
• Pimsiri Petchnamrob convicted for 2020 speech criticizing monarchy's unchecked power and UN statement on lese majeste law
• Arnon Nampa, Somyot Pruksakaseksuk, and Promsorn Viradhammajari also sentenced for criticizing King Maha Vajiralongkorn
• Sentences reduced from 4 years due to 'useful testimony'; Pimsiri granted $4,800 bail pending appeal
• Thailand has prosecuted 1,987 people for free expression since 2014 coup, with 285 facing lese majeste charges
• Lese majeste law carries 3-15 years prison per offense under Penal Code section 112
• UN human rights experts called for charges to be dropped; Working Group on Arbitrary Detention demanded Arnon's release

items.title: Ethiopia Blocks Major Social Media Platforms During Religious Protests
items.summary: Ethiopia restricted Facebook, Telegram, TikTok and YouTube on state ISP Ethio Telecom during anti-government protests over Ethiopian Orthodox Church tensions, with NetBlocks confirming circumvention via VPN services.
items.content_text:
NetBlocks confirmed restriction of Facebook, Telegram, TikTok on Feb 9, 2023, YouTube added Feb 10
• Restrictions implemented on state internet provider Ethio Telecom amid religious protests
• Protests sparked by government attempt to split Ethiopian Orthodox Tewhado Church (EOTC)
• Users migrated to YouTube after initial platforms blocked, prompting additional restrictions
• NetBlocks measured disruption from 20 vantage points showing targeted platform blocking
• VPN services confirmed as effective circumvention method for these restrictions
• Pattern consistent with Ethiopia's history of social media blocks during protests
• Authorities typically implement restrictions without public announcement

items.title: Wisconsin Removes VPN Ban from Age Verification Bill After Pushback
items.summary: Wisconsin lawmakers removed VPN bans from age verification bill S.B. 130/A.B. 105 after widespread opposition, though EFF still opposes the bill awaiting Governor Evers' signature.
items.content_text:
Wisconsin removed VPN ban provision from S.B. 130/A.B. 105 on February 25, 2026 after pushback
• Bill still requires invasive age verification for websites with content deemed 'sexual'
• EFF continues opposing the bill despite VPN provision removal
• Original bill would have forced websites to block all VPN users to comply with Wisconsin law
• Technical impossibility of geo-blocking VPN users would lead to over-blocking or nationwide restrictions
• Bill expands definition of 'harmful to minors' beyond historically narrow court-recognized categories
• Age verification requirements create privacy nightmare requiring government IDs and biometric data
• Governor Tony Evers urged to veto the bill despite VPN provision removal

items.title: North Korea Escalates Tech Surveillance and Info Controls Targeting Youth
items.summary: North Korea's 2026 Party Congress coincides with intensified crackdowns on tech-savvy youth accessing foreign media, with new laws criminalizing information access and public executions for sharing content.
items.content_text:
North Korea's Workers' Party Congress convenes amid escalating repression since 2021
• Government specifically targets 'tech-savvy young people' for surveillance and punishment
• Three new laws criminalize foreign media access, South Korean expressions, and 'non-socialist' cultural practices
• Punishments range from forced labor to execution - witness reports 22-year-old executed for sharing foreign media in 2022
• Kim Jong Un praised youth who sacrificed 'precious dreams' for state demands, including military deployment to Ukraine
• Crackdowns focus on information control through schools, workplaces, and party youth organizations
• UN reports worsening repression over past decade, with forced labor potentially constituting crimes against humanity
• Human Rights Watch calls for ending criminal penalties for information access and independent monitoring

items.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Webinar Series for Civil Society
items.summary: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline launches 'Safe and Strong' webinar series targeting spear-phishing threats against activists, journalists, and civil society with expert guidance from Citizen Lab and security researchers.
items.content_text:
Access Now launching 'Safe and Strong' webinar series on digital security for civil society
• First session March 10, 2026 focuses on spear-phishing attacks targeting activists and journalists
• Expert speakers include Citizen Lab's Rebekah Brown, Lookout's Justin Albrecht, and regional digital security specialists
• Content draws from documented attacks in Eastern Europe and Middle East regions
• Initiative aims to share actionable insights as digital attacks against civil society grow more complex
• Webinar targets high-profile activists, journalists, and NGO members who face sophisticated social engineering
• Event brings together civil society, private sector, and support organizations for resilience building

items.title: OONI Launches 2025 Fundraising Campaign Amid Global Censorship Surge
items.summary: Internet freedom measurement organization OONI launches fundraising campaign as they document increasing censorship of social media, messaging apps, and news sites across 10+ countries in 2024.
items.content_text:
OONI (Open Observatory of Network Interference) launched end-of-year fundraising campaign
• Organization documented surge in internet censorship globally throughout 2024
• Social media blocks observed in Togo, Cameroon, and Tanzania
• Messaging apps (Telegram, WhatsApp) blocked in Kenya, Vietnam, Nepal, and Iran
• Independent news media websites blocked in India, Egypt, and Jordan
• Censorship often coincides with protests and elections, limiting critical communication
• OONI provides essential measurement tools for documenting network interference

items.title: EFF Warns of Growing Age Verification Mandates and Facial Recognition Surveillance
items.summary: EFF highlights escalating threats to online privacy including Discord's mandatory age verification, Meta's face-scanning smart glasses, and surveillance tech normalization.
items.content_text:
Discord implements mandatory age verification despite recent data breach [EFF]
• Leaked Meta memo reveals plans for face-scanning smart glasses technology
• Ring's Super Bowl ad openly promoted surveillance capabilities to consumers
• Age verification laws increasingly require ID presentation to access online content
• EFF warns these measures threaten privacy and free speech for all users, not just minors
• Pattern shows normalization of invasive identity verification across platforms
• Technical implementation creates honeypots of sensitive personal data

items.title: FBI Counterterrorism Unit Targets Climate Activists Under Trump's NSPM-7 Directive
items.summary: FBI counterterrorism agents visited former Extinction Rebellion member's home for questioning, marking escalation in Trump administration's targeting of activist groups as domestic terrorists under NSPM-7.
items.content_text:
FBI Joint Terrorism Task Force agents questioned former Extinction Rebellion NYC member at his home
• Agents traveled hundreds of miles and made multiple contact attempts over weeks
• Investigation linked to Trump's NSPM-7 directive targeting progressive groups for 'anti-fascism' beliefs
• Similar visits occurred to climate activists in Boston area in March 2024
• Internal FBI report shows active NSPM-7 investigations in 27 locations including New York
• Extinction Rebellion NYC conducts only public, non-violent direct actions typically charged as misdemeanors
• Assistant AG Harmeet Dhillon threatened investigation after group protested Rep. Suozzi's ICE funding vote
• Pattern suggests systematic federal surveillance of climate and anti-ICE activists nationwide

items.title: OONI Establishes Foundation, Advances Global Censorship Detection Tools in 2024
items.summary: OONI launched a new foundation and significantly upgraded its censorship detection toolkit with OONI Run v2, multiplatform apps, and enhanced data analysis capabilities to support global internet freedom monitoring.
items.content_text:
Open Observatory of Network Interference Foundation (OONI) ETS officially established in Rome, Italy in February 2024
• Launched OONI Run v2 for coordinated censorship measurement campaigns
• Developing multiplatform OONI Probe app to expand measurement coverage
• Enhanced OONI Explorer with thematic censorship findings presentation
• Introduced new experiments and expanded testing model for richer input analysis
• Advanced data analysis methods for better censorship detection accuracy
• Conducted OONI Partner Gathering 2024 in Malaysia with global research community
• Published research reports and censorship findings accessible via OONI Explorer
• Expanded localization efforts including Test Lists Editor and Outreach Kit
• Strengthened partnerships and conducted workshops globally to build measurement capacity

items.title: Brazil Blocks Telegram Over Refusal to Provide User Data on Far-Right Groups
items.summary: Brazil restricted Telegram on major ISPs after the messaging platform refused court orders to provide user data from alleged far-right groups planning school attacks.
items.content_text:
NetBlocks confirmed Telegram restriction on major Brazilian ISPs Claro and Vivo as of April 27, 2023
• Court ordered suspension after Telegram failed to provide complete user data from two group chats allegedly planning school attacks
• Federal Police requested data as part of investigations into far-right and neo-Nazi online activity
• Service remains accessible on smaller networks and via VPN circumvention
• Brazil has history of Telegram restrictions (2015, 2016) despite generally open internet policy
• Block affects Telegram frontends and backends on networks AS28573 and AS18881
• NetBlocks opposes messaging restrictions due to disproportionate impact on public communication rights

items.title: EFF Backs State Laws to Sue Federal Agents for Constitutional Violations
items.summary: EFF supports California S.B. 747 and similar state laws enabling lawsuits against federal agents who violate constitutional rights, as Supreme Court has gutted federal protections while ICE/CBP violate filming rights.
items.content_text:
ICE/CBP agents are violating First Amendment right to record law enforcement, including before killing Alex Pretti and Renee Good in Minnesota
• Supreme Court has systematically weakened Bivens doctrine that allowed suing federal agents for constitutional violations since 1971
• No federal statute exists to sue federal officials (unlike 42 U.S.C. § 1983 for state/local officials from Reconstruction era)
• California S.B. 747 ('No Kings Act') would create state-level remedy to sue federal agents for constitutional violations
• EFF argues state laws don't violate Supremacy Clause because they enforce federal constitutional rights
• Legal accountability systems encourage agents to follow law while impunity encourages violations
• Model legislation from Protect Democracy called 'Universal Constitutional Remedies Act' spreading to other states

items.title: OONI Develops Anonymous Credential System for Censorship Measurement Privacy
items.summary: OONI is integrating anonymous credentials into OONI Probe to increase trust in censorship measurements while preserving user privacy and anonymity.
items.content_text:
OONI developing anonymous credential system for OONI Probe censorship measurement tool
• Goal is to enrich measurement data with probe properties while preserving user privacy
• System aims to increase trust in OONI's global censorship measurement dataset
• Part of broader effort to enhance security without requiring user identification
• OONI published comprehensive academic literature review of anonymous credential systems
• Critical for protecting activists and researchers measuring internet censorship
• Could set precedent for privacy-preserving verification in freedom tech tools

items.title: Pakistan Jails Human Rights Lawyers 17 Years for Tweets Criticizing Military
items.summary: Pakistan sentenced two human rights lawyers to 17 years in prison for tweets criticizing military abuses in Balochistan, using cybercrime laws to prosecute digital dissent.
items.content_text:
Imaan Mazari and Hadi Ali Chattha sentenced to 17 years under Pakistan's Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act (PECA)
• Charged for 2021-2025 tweets criticizing military operations in Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces
• Trial moved unusually fast - indictment to sentencing in under 3 months vs typical years-long timeline
• Evidence consisted of tweet screenshots and witness statements; defense denied adequate cross-examination
• PECA originally designed for cybercrime now weaponized against journalists, activists, and opposition members
• Sections 9, 10, 11, and 26A cited - including 'cyber terrorism' and 'false information' provisions with vague definitions
• UN Human Rights Office and EU condemned sentencing as judicial persecution
• Pakistan bar associations staged three-day strike; Amnesty called it 'miscarriage of justice'

items.title: Iran Implements Complete Internet Shutdown During Protests, 2,403+ Killed
items.summary: Iran shut down all internet and mobile connectivity nationwide on Jan 8, 2026 amid currency protests. Over 2,400 protesters killed, 18,000+ arrested as authorities jam even satellite internet to conceal violence.
items.content_text:
Complete nationwide internet shutdown began Jan 8, 2026 following currency collapse protests starting Dec 28, 2025
• Cloudflare data confirms internet traffic dropped to near zero at 18:45 UTC on Jan 8
• At least 2,403 protesters killed and 18,000+ arrested as of Jan 13, 2026 [HRANA]
• Authorities initially throttled internet by 35% and blocked circumvention tools before total shutdown
• Satellite internet services also disrupted through signal jamming and equipment confiscation
• Tech activists and internet freedom community achieved limited connectivity restoration during shutdown
• Pattern matches previous Iran shutdowns during 2019 and 2022 protests that preceded mass violence
• Access Now and 40+ organizations demand immediate restoration and accountability

items.title: Tails 7.0 RC2 Released with Debian 13, Updated Privacy Tools
items.summary: Tails privacy OS releases second release candidate based on Debian 13, updating core freedom tech tools like Tor, Electrum Bitcoin wallet, and OnionShare file sharing.
items.content_text:
Tails 7.0~rc2 is second release candidate, official release planned September 18 or October 16
• First Tails version based on Debian 13 (Trixie) and GNOME 48
• Increases minimum RAM requirements from 2GB to 3GB (affects <2% of users)
• Updates Tor client to 0.4.8.17 and Linux kernel to 6.12.41 for better hardware support
• Updates Electrum Bitcoin wallet from 4.3.4 to 4.5.8
• Updates OnionShare anonymous file sharing from 2.6.2 to 2.6.3
• Updates KeePassXC password manager from 2.7.4 to 2.7.10
• Removes aircrack-ng wireless security tool and other legacy software
• Known issue: slower boot times to be fixed in final release

items.title: OONI and Digitally Right Document Network Shutdowns During Bangladesh Protests
items.summary: OONI collaborated with local digital rights group Digitally Right to document internet shutdowns and censorship during recent Bangladesh protests, providing crucial evidence of network interference.
items.content_text:
Open Observatory of Network Interference (OONI) partnered with Bangladesh-based Digitally Right
• Study documented network interference and shutdowns during Bangladesh protests
• Research involved Network Measurement Fellows tracking disruptions across Bangladesh
• Key contributors include Tohidul Islam Raso (Digitally Right) and Maria Xynou (OONI)
• Journalist Suhadha Afrin from Prothom Alo contributed to research and documentation
• Study combines technical measurement data with on-ground reporting
• Operators and service providers provided interviews and information
• Report provides evidence base for documenting government censorship tactics

items.title: CPJ Report: 58 of 59 Palestinian Journalists Report Torture in Israeli Detention
items.summary: Committee to Protect Journalists releasing report Feb 19 documenting systematic torture claims from 58 of 59 Palestinian journalists detained by Israel since Oct 2023.
items.content_text:
CPJ conducted in-depth interviews with 59 Palestinian journalists released since October 7, 2023
• 58 of 59 journalists reported experiencing torture, abuse, or violence in Israeli custody
• Alleged abuses include beatings, starvation, medical neglect, sexual violence including rape
• Journalists describe stress positions, prolonged high-volume sound exposure, and threats
• CPJ reviewed supporting evidence including photographs, medical reports, and legal documents
• Systematic targeting of journalists represents critical threat to information freedom
• Report demonstrates how physical suppression of media workers enables information control
• Pattern suggests coordinated effort to silence Palestinian media coverage

items.title: Tails 7.4 Adds Persistent Language Settings, Drops BitTorrent Support
items.summary: Tails 7.4 introduces persistent language/keyboard settings and removes BitTorrent downloads due to security concerns with v1 to v2 transition.
items.content_text:
New feature allows saving language and keyboard layout settings to USB stick for automatic application on restart
• Language/keyboard data stored unencrypted on USB to enable easier Persistent Storage passphrase entry
• Updated Tor Browser to 15.0.4 and Thunderbird to 140.6.0 for latest security patches
• Linux kernel updated to 6.12.63 for improved hardware support and security
• BitTorrent download support removed due to security concerns with v1 format during transition to v2
• Fixed critical bugs including VeraCrypt desktop crashes and Kleopatra GPG file handling
• Automatic upgrades available from Tails 7.0+ with manual upgrade fallback option

items.title: Signal Announces Quantum-Resistant Protocol Upgrade (PQXDH)
items.summary: Signal Protocol introduces PQXDH upgrade, adding quantum-resistant cryptography layer to protect against future quantum computers that could break current encryption.
items.content_text:
Signal Protocol announces first quantum resistance upgrade called PQXDH
• Upgrades existing X3DH key agreement protocol with quantum-resistant protections
• Signal Protocol used by billions globally across Signal and other messaging platforms
• Addresses threat of future quantum computers breaking current encryption standards
• Technical whitepaper and specifications published for developer implementation
• Represents proactive step toward post-quantum cryptography for secure communications
• Critical for long-term protection of activist and dissident communications

items.title: OONI Launches Enhanced Censorship Detection Platform, Seeks Community Feedback
items.summary: OONI has released major platform upgrades including new censorship findings tools and improved methodologies, and is requesting community feedback to evaluate impact.
items.content_text:
OONI released new Explorer Censorship Findings platform for better censorship event monitoring
• Added thematic censorship pages for targeted analysis of specific censorship patterns
• Published new research reports based on OONI measurement data
• Implemented improved throttling detection methodology for more accurate measurements
• Deployed OONI Pipeline v5 with enhanced data analysis capabilities
• Seeking community feedback to evaluate impact and guide future improvements
• Enhancements aim to better support internet freedom community responses to censorship

items.title: Silent.Link: Bitcoin-Native eSIM Service Bypasses SIM-Swap Attacks and State Censorship
items.summary: Silent.Link offers privacy-focused eSIM data/SMS services paid with Bitcoin, eliminating SIM-swap risks while bypassing state firewalls in China and Dubai's VoIP restrictions.
items.content_text:
Silent.Link provides global eSIM-only mobile data and SMS services paid with Bitcoin
• No personal information collected - users can purchase without even providing email
• Authentication via backup codes (similar to Bitcoin seed phrases) eliminates SIM-swap attack vectors
• Reportedly bypasses Chinese firewall and Dubai VoIP restrictions
• Founded by Bitcoin community members inspired by Matt Odell, profitable and self-funded
• Automated roaming across countries without carrier negotiations or overcharges
• No outgoing SMS or voice calls - focuses on data and incoming authentication messages
• Built on BTCPay Server stack with Lightning support for Bitcoin-native payments

items.title: Open Measurement Gathering Hosts Public AMA on Internet Censorship Detection Tools
items.summary: Key internet measurement organizations (OONI, Censored Planet, IODA, Measurement Lab) held public AMA sharing project updates and future plans for detecting censorship and network interference.
items.content_text:
Open Measurement Gathering (OMG) held public virtual event June 25, 2025
• Featured four major internet measurement organizations: OONI, Censored Planet, IODA, and Measurement Lab
• Each group presented 30-minute updates on platforms, tools, and datasets
• Event aimed to gather community feedback on censorship detection infrastructure
• Third OMG convening, following two previous private meetings with public reports
• Organizations provide critical data on internet shutdowns and censorship globally
• Community engagement suggests coordination among measurement infrastructure providers

items.title: Meta Considers Facial Recognition for Smart Glasses Despite $7B Privacy Settlement History
items.summary: Meta is reportedly planning facial recognition for smart glasses, timing launch during political distractions. EFF warns of mass surveillance risks and billions in legal costs.
items.content_text:
Meta considering facial recognition for smart glasses according to internal documents [New York Times]
• Company plans launch "during dynamic political environment" to avoid civil society scrutiny
• Would require collecting faceprints from everyone in camera view without consent
• Meta previously paid nearly $7 billion in facial recognition privacy settlements
• Dozens of state laws require affirmative consent for biometric data collection
• EFF warns of mass surveillance, data breach, and discrimination risks
• Public backlash already seen with ICE's Mobile Fortify and Amazon Ring facial recognition

items.title: Gabon Implements Indefinite Social Media Shutdown Amid Anti-Government Protests
items.summary: Gabon's communications authority ordered indefinite social media blocking of Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram and YouTube during ongoing protests, with #KeepItOn coalition demanding immediate restoration.
items.content_text:
Gabon's High Authority for Communication ordered indefinite social media shutdown on February 17, 2026
• OONI confirms blocking of Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube as of February 18, 2026
• Shutdown coincides with ongoing anti-government protests and labor strikes
• Government claims measures prevent 'spread of false information' and protect national unity
• Coalition of 345+ human rights organizations from 105 countries demands immediate reversal
• Gabon has history of shutdowns: 2023 elections, 2021 COVID protests, 2019 coup attempt, 2016 protests
• ISPs Moov Gabon, Gabon Telecom, and Airtel Gabon called to refuse enforcement of shutdown orders
• Pattern demonstrates recurring use of digital repression during political instability

items.title: OONI Documents Ongoing X/Twitter Blocking Across Tanzania Networks
items.summary: Technical measurements confirm X (Twitter) has been blocked across major Tanzanian ISPs since May 2025, with network-level censorship affecting multiple autonomous systems.
items.content_text:
OONI data confirms X/Twitter blocking across Tanzania since May 2025
• Censorship implemented across major ISPs: Airtel Tanzania, Vodacom, Tanzania Telecom, and Zanzibar Connections
• Technical analysis shows DNS and IP-based blocking methods being deployed
• Network variances suggest coordinated but technically different implementation approaches
• Blocking affects both twitter.com and x.com domains
• Government censorship targets major social media platform used for information sharing
• OONI's measurements provide technical evidence for circumvention tool development

items.title: 40 Uyghurs Forcibly Returned to China by Thailand Remain Missing After One Year
items.summary: Thailand deported 40 Uyghur men to China in February 2025 despite torture risks. Their whereabouts remain unknown, constituting forced disappearances under international law.
items.content_text:
Thai authorities forcibly returned 40 Uyghur men to China in February 2025 after decade in detention
• Men had fled systematic persecution in Xinjiang and faced grave risks of torture upon return
• Deportation violated international nonrefoulement principle prohibiting return to persecution
• Family members abroad have received no contact since deportation occurred
• Chinese government's refusal to report their fate constitutes forced disappearances under international law
• UNHCR was denied access to assess refugee status before deportation
• Pattern of Uyghur deportations documented across multiple countries under Chinese pressure
• Highlights critical need for secure communications and identity protection tools for at-risk populations

items.title: Journalist Safety Expert Highlights Digital Security Tools for High-Risk Environments
items.summary: Free Press Unlimited's Pablo Arcuri outlines digital security priorities for journalists, emphasizing phone protection and tools like VPNs while connecting online disinformation to physical violence.
items.content_text:
Pablo Arcuri from Free Press Unlimited interviewed on multi-dimensional threats to journalists
• Primary digital advice: treat phones as most sensitive equipment, avoid suspicious apps/links
• State-sponsored spyware like Pegasus still often requires user interaction to deploy
• Recommends VPNs, password managers, and Totem Project for journalist digital safety training
• SLAPPs (Strategic Lawsuits Against Public Participation) used as harassment tool in democracies
• Online disinformation campaigns serve as precursors to real-world violence against journalists
• 90% global impunity rate for journalist murders according to UNESCO data
• Women journalists face disproportionate gender-based online harassment and physical threats

items.title: UK Court Orders Saudi Arabia to Pay £3m to Dissident Targeted with Pegasus Spyware
items.summary: UK court awarded £3m to Saudi dissident Ghanem Al-Masarir after Citizen Lab proved his phones were infected with NSO Group's Pegasus spyware by Saudi operators, setting precedent for transnational surveillance accountability.
items.content_text:
UK court ordered Saudi Arabia to pay £3m to London-based dissident Ghanem Al-Masarir in January 2026
• Al-Masarir's iPhones were infected with NSO Group's Pegasus spyware by Saudi operator 'KINGDOM'
• Citizen Lab researcher Bill Marczak provided key forensic evidence proving spyware infection
• Damages awarded for psychological harm, lost earnings, and physical assault by Saudi agents
• Court judgment establishes legal precedent for accountability in transnational digital repression
• Case demonstrates sophisticated state-sponsored surveillance targeting dissidents abroad
• Citizen Lab initially discovered KINGDOM's targeting of Saudi dissidents in 2018
• Al-Masarir is a Saudi activist and political YouTuber operating from London

items.title: Uganda Faces Election Internet Shutdown Threat Despite Coalition Warning
items.summary: Digital rights coalition warns Uganda's government against internet shutdowns ahead of 2026 elections, citing escalating media crackdown and concerning signals including VPN threats and Starlink blocking directive.
items.content_text:
345-org #KeepItOn coalition appeals to President Museveni to maintain internet access during January 15, 2026 elections
• Uganda Communications Commission (UCC) threatened VPN users accessing blocked Facebook platform
• UCC directed Starlink to disable services weeks before elections, raising connectivity concerns
• Electoral Commission officials suggested internet shutdown may be necessary due to 'uncontrollable' misinformation
• Government escalating crackdown includes journalist accreditation revocations at NTV Uganda and Daily Monitor
• Uganda has history of election shutdowns: 4-day blackout in 2021, social media blocks in 2016
• Facebook remains blocked since 2021 elections; government previously blocked 100+ VPNs
• Pattern indicates high likelihood of digital restrictions during politically sensitive period

items.title: MTN Congo Accused of Enabling State Surveillance in Gang Crackdown
items.summary: Access Now alleges MTN Congo sent mass SMS bounty messages for security forces targeting suspected gang members, potentially violating human rights commitments and data protection laws.
items.content_text:
Congo's Presidential Security (DGSP) conducting crackdown on 'Kuluna' gang members since September 2024, reportedly with extrajudicial killings
• MTN Congo allegedly sent mass SMS messages offering rewards up to $1,760 USD for information on suspected gang members
• Access Now claims this violates MTN's UN Global Compact commitments and Global Network Initiative membership obligations
• MTN Congo has 3.3M subscribers (over half the market), making mass surveillance capability significant
• Alleged violations of Congo's 2019 data protection law requiring impact assessments for high-risk processing
• DGSP operating outside legal mandate - tasked with presidential security, not public order maintenance
• Human rights groups CAD and OCDH documented extrajudicial killings during the operation
• Access Now demanding independent investigation into MTN's data sharing practices with authorities

items.title: Kimwolf Botnet Sybil Attack Disrupts I2P Anonymity Network
items.summary: The massive Kimwolf IoT botnet accidentally crippled I2P's anonymous communications network by flooding it with 700k infected devices attempting to use it as fallback C2 infrastructure.
items.content_text:
Kimwolf botnet attempted to join 700,000 infected IoT devices to I2P network as backup command and control infrastructure
• Mass influx overwhelmed I2P's ~15,000-20,000 daily users, causing widespread service disruptions since Feb 3
• Attack constitutes a Sybil attack - single entity disrupting peer-to-peer network with massive fake identities
• I2P network operating at half capacity, with new stability improvements rolling out
• Botmasters acknowledged accident in Discord channel, seeking takedown-resistant C2 alternatives
• Similar experiments reportedly conducted with Tor network without major disruptions
• Incident highlights vulnerability of decentralized networks to resource exhaustion attacks
• Botnet recently lost competent operators, leading to rookie mistakes and 600k+ device losses

items.title: Tails 7.4.2 Emergency Release Fixes Critical Kernel Vulnerabilities
items.summary: Tails issued emergency update fixing Linux kernel vulnerabilities that could allow privilege escalation and potential deanonymization of users.
items.content_text:
Emergency release addresses DSA 6126-1, multiple critical Linux kernel security vulnerabilities
• Vulnerabilities could allow applications to gain admin privileges and potentially deanonymize users
• Linux kernel updated to version 6.12.69 to patch security flaws
• Attack vector requires chaining with other exploits, making it complex but possible for advanced adversaries
• Tails team notes governments or hacking firms could potentially exploit these vulnerabilities
• Additional fixes include Wi-Fi settings, Electrum wallet crashes, and language persistence issues
• Automatic upgrades available from Tails 7.0+, manual upgrade option provided for compatibility issues

items.title: Proton Guide: Technical Tools to Resist AI Training Data Scraping
items.summary: Privacy company Proton outlines practical methods for creators to protect their work from AI training scraping, including pixel-level cloaking tools, data poisoning techniques, and GDPR opt-outs.
items.content_text:
Major AI companies like OpenAI and Meta scrape public internet content without creator consent for model training
• Meta admits to scraping all public Facebook/Instagram posts back to 2007 for AI training
• Technical protection tools available: Glaze (pixel cloaking) and Nightshade (data poisoning)
• Glaze makes subtle pixel changes that confuse AI models while remaining invisible to humans
• Nightshade introduces poison data causing AI to mislearn associations (cars as cats, etc.)
• Have I Been Trained website lets creators check if work appears in known AI datasets
• EU residents can leverage GDPR to demand exclusion from AI training datasets
• Research shows current protections may weaken as AI systems evolve
• Double standard: AI companies claim fair use for scraping but treat outputs as proprietary

items.title: Mauritania Imposed 6-Day Mobile Internet Shutdown After Prison Break
items.summary: Mauritania cut mobile internet nationwide for 6 days following a violent prison escape, affecting businesses and demonstrating how infrastructure-level shutdowns bypass VPN protections.
items.content_text:
Four high-risk prisoners escaped Nouakchott Civil Prison on March 5, 2023, killing two guards
• Government responded with nation-scale mobile internet shutdown lasting 6 days (March 6-12)
• Fixed-line internet remained available while mobile data was completely cut
• VPN services ineffective against this infrastructure-level disruption method
• Local businesses severely impacted, especially those relying on mobile payment apps
• NetBlocks confirmed shutdown using technical measurement data from Google Transparency
• Mauritania has history of election-related internet shutdowns (June 2019)
• Service restored after authorities reported fugitives killed or captured

items.title: Ethiopia Revokes Reuters Credentials After Sudan Training Camp Investigation
items.summary: Ethiopia revoked Reuters journalists' accreditation following report alleging secret UAE-financed camp training Sudan's RSF fighters, part of broader media crackdown ahead of June elections.
items.content_text:
Ethiopian Media Authority revoked accreditation of three Reuters journalists on February 14, 2026
• Action followed Reuters February 10 investigation alleging Ethiopia hosts UAE-financed camp training Sudan's RSF fighters
• Reuters barred from covering African Union summit in Addis Ababa
• Part of escalating press restrictions including suspension of Deutsche Welle correspondents in October
• Wazema Radio's license revoked in January for reporting against 'national interest'
• Crackdown intensifying ahead of June 2026 general election amid ongoing regional conflicts
• Pattern demonstrates government using regulatory power to silence critical reporting on sensitive geopolitical activities

items.title: Wisconsin VPN Ban Bill Passes Assembly, Senate Vote Imminent
items.summary: Wisconsin's S.B. 130/A.B. 105 effectively bans VPNs and mandates invasive age verification for websites, passed State Assembly and faces Senate vote tomorrow.
items.content_text:
Wisconsin S.B. 130/A.B. 105 passed State Assembly, Senate vote scheduled for tomorrow
• Bill requires websites to block VPN users and implement invasive age verification
• Targets websites with content lawmakers deem 'sexual' with broad, vague definitions
• EFF sent letter to entire Wisconsin Legislature urging rejection of the bill
• Technical enforcement is unworkable - sites can't reliably determine VPN user location
• Creates privacy nightmare requiring government IDs, financial data, biometric identifiers
• Broadens 'harmful to minors' definition beyond court-recognized categories
• Forces websites to choose: over-block VPNs, block all Wisconsin users, or implement nationwide restrictions

items.title: OONI Publishes Complete Security Audit Report for Censorship Measurement Tools
items.summary: OONI publicly released a comprehensive security audit report of its internet censorship measurement platform, conducted by Radically Open Security with OTF support.
items.content_text:
OONI published full penetration test report conducted by Radically Open Security
• Security audit ran from August 5 to October 18, 2024 with Open Technology Fund support
• Crystal box penetration test evaluated core components of OONI's censorship measurement software
• Complete transparency approach - full detailed report made publicly available
• Audit focused on ensuring high security standards for tools used by activists and researchers
• Part of OONI's broader commitment to secure, high-quality free software development
• Critical for user safety given OONI's use in repressive environments worldwide

items.title: Trump Admin Cuts Freedom Tech Funding While Launching 'Anti-Censorship' Portal
items.summary: Trump administration launches freedom.gov to bypass European regulations while simultaneously cutting funding to organizations that build censorship circumvention tools and support at-risk activists globally.
items.content_text:
Trump administration launching freedom.gov portal to help users bypass European content restrictions
• US foreign aid cuts eliminated funding for organizations developing censorship circumvention tools
• Open Observatory of Network Interference reports reduced ability to monitor internet censorship
• South Asian digital rights org lost 45% of budget, threatens rapid response helpline for at-risk users
• State Department threatening visa denials for researchers and fact-checkers over 'censorship' claims
• Five Europeans already banned for allegedly pressuring tech firms to 'censor American viewpoints'
• Policy contradicts stated goal of supporting free expression and anti-censorship efforts
• Funding cuts particularly impact organizations supporting journalists and dissidents in repressive environments

items.title: New York Proposes Mandatory 3D Printer Surveillance for Firearm Detection
items.summary: New York's budget bill would require all 3D printers sold in the state to include 'blocking technology' that scans files for potential firearm blueprints and refuses to print flagged items.
items.content_text:
NY budget bill S.9005/A.10005 mandates 'blocking technology' in all 3D printers sold in state
• Required software/firmware must scan print files with 'firearms blueprint detection algorithm'
• Printers would refuse to print anything flagged as potential firearm or component
• Bruce Schneier warns this creates same problems as DRM - controlling general-purpose computers
• Measure targets maker community, educators, and small manufacturers statewide
• Technical approach fundamentally flawed - algorithms can be bypassed or circumvented
• Creates precedent for government-mandated surveillance in manufacturing tools
• Threatens user sovereignty over personally-owned devices and equipment

items.title: OONI Launches Community Survey to Improve Censorship Measurement Tools
items.summary: OONI, the global internet censorship measurement network, is surveying its 50+ partner organizations and hundreds of thousands of users to improve their tools and outreach.
items.content_text:
OONI (Open Observatory of Network Interference) launched community survey
• Network includes over 50 partner organizations worldwide
• Hundreds of thousands of OONI Probe users contribute measurements globally
• Survey aims to improve outreach, tools, and documentation
• Community contributes through censorship measurements, localization, and research
• Feedback will inform development of critical internet freedom infrastructure
• OONI remains primary source for documenting global internet censorship

items.title: OONI Explorer Launches Thematic Censorship Tracking Pages
items.summary: OONI Explorer released new dedicated pages tracking global censorship of social media, news media, and circumvention tools with charts and data-driven reports.
items.content_text:
OONI Explorer launched three new thematic censorship pages with interactive charts
• New pages track blocking of social media, news media, and circumvention tools globally
• Data comes from OONI's measurement network - the world's largest open censorship dataset
• Pages provide country-specific blocking data and trends over time
• Tool enables researchers and activists to identify censorship patterns
• Represents significant improvement in accessibility of censorship intelligence
• Previously this data required manual analysis of raw OONI measurements

items.title: EFF Releases Guide for Journalists to Investigate Police Surveillance Tech
items.summary: EFF, Center for Just Journalism, and IPVM released 'Selling Safety' report to help journalists cut through police tech marketing hype and report accurately on surveillance tools' costs, effectiveness, and privacy impacts.
items.content_text:
New 'Selling Safety' report published by EFF, Center for Just Journalism, and IPVM
• Guide helps journalists investigate police surveillance technology claims and marketing
• Report exposes how police tech companies manufacture appearance of effectiveness without measurement
• Industry described as 'unregulated, unexamined, and consequential' with profit motive driving adoption
• Provides tools for journalists to ask better questions about costs, privacy, and accountability
• Addresses pattern of unsubstantiated claims from surveillance technology vendors
• Complements EFF's existing Street-Level Surveillance hub and Atlas of Surveillance resources

items.title: North Korea Escalates Surveillance and Punishment of Tech-Savvy Youth
items.summary: Human Rights Watch reports North Korea has intensified surveillance targeting young people with technical skills, with punishments including execution for accessing foreign media or sharing content.
items.content_text:
North Korea's 9th Party Congress convenes amid escalated repression focused on young people since 2021
• Tech-savvy youth specifically targeted for surveillance according to defector testimony [Kim Il-hyuk, rice trader]
• Public execution documented of 22-year-old man in 2022 for listening to South Korean songs and sharing foreign media
• Multiple laws criminalize foreign information access with punishments from forced labor to execution
• Reactionary Ideology and Culture Rejection Law bans watching/sharing foreign media and 'non-socialist' cultural expression
• Government mobilizes unpaid forced labor for Party Congress preparations through quotas on workers and students
• Songbun caste system compounds repression by limiting access to resources based on political reliability
• UN reports North Korean repression has worsened over past decade, with forced labor potentially constituting crimes against humanity

items.title: Rights Groups Demand Accountability from AppLogic Networks for Egypt Censorship Infrastructure
items.summary: Human rights coalition demands transparency from AppLogic Networks (formerly Sandvine) over withdrawal from Egypt, where its deep packet inspection tech blocked 630+ websites including independent media outlets.
items.content_text:
10 human rights orgs demand AppLogic Networks provide evidence of complete withdrawal from Egypt by Feb 26, 2026
• AppLogic's deep packet inspection technology used to block 630+ websites in Egypt, targeting independent media
• Company committed to exiting Egypt by Dec 31, 2025 as condition for removal from US Commerce Entity List
• Recent blocks continue: Brussels-based Zawia3 outlet blocked Feb 20, 2025, along with backup domain
• Technical research from Citizen Lab and Egyptian orgs like Masaar documented mass censorship traceable to AppLogic tech
• AppLogic cancelled engagement meetings with Egyptian civil society 'at last minute' citing vague safety concerns
• Coalition questions whether promised 1% profit donations for remediation reached affected Egyptian organizations
• Demands include evidence of hardware removal, plans for stakeholder engagement, and disclosure of remediation funds

items.title: EFF Releases Guide to Expose Police Surveillance Tech Sales Hype
items.summary: EFF, CJJ, and IPVM released 'Selling Safety' report helping journalists critically examine police surveillance technology claims and expose industry tactics that exploit taxpayers while eroding civil liberties.
items.content_text:
EFF, Center for Just Journalism, and IPVM released joint 'Selling Safety' report for journalists
• Guide helps cut through surveillance tech marketing claims that are 'often misleading'
• Police tech industry described as 'unregulated, unexamined, and consequential' with billion-dollar profit motives
• Companies manufacture appearance of effectiveness rather than measuring actual results
• Report provides tools for better questions, understanding incentives, and finding accountability stories
• Surveillance technologies spreading faster than public understanding or oversight
• EFF maintains Street-Level Surveillance hub and Atlas of Surveillance mapping tools
• Resource aims to protect taxpayer dollars and civil liberties through critical journalism

items.title: Tor Project Launches 'Free the Internet' Fundraising Campaign with Matched Donations
items.summary: Tor Project announces fundraising campaign highlighting real-world censorship resistance use cases in Turkmenistan, Russia, and Egypt, with donations matched 1:1 up to $250K.
items.content_text:
Tor Project launches 'FREE THE INTERNET' fundraising campaign with Power Up Privacy matching donations 1:1 up to $250,000
• Turkmen.news uses Tor to provide safe internet access in heavily censored Turkmenistan
• Freedom of the Press Foundation relies on Tor as backbone for SecureDrop journalist communication tool
• Users in Russia and Egypt access encrypted email services like Tuta Mail via Tor Browser to bypass blocks
• Campaign runs for three months with merchandise incentives for donations over $25
• Two 'State of the Onion' virtual events scheduled for November 12 and December 10
• December 10 event coincides with UN Universal Declaration of Human Rights anniversary

items.title: Philippines 'Red-Tags' Environmental Groups, Freezes Bank Accounts of Solar/Disaster Relief NGOs
items.summary: Philippine government systematically labels environmental defenders as 'terrorists,' freezing bank accounts of NGOs providing solar power and disaster relief to rural communities.
items.content_text:
Government task forces accuse environmental NGOs of terrorism/communist links through 'red-tagging' practice
• Leyte Center for Development (LCDe) had foreign donor accounts frozen, director faces terrorism financing charges
• Sibat nonprofit installing solar-powered water pumps also red-tagged and harassed by military
• UN experts confirm systematic targeting of environmental defenders, Indigenous peoples, and humanitarian workers
• Financial censorship prevents crucial disaster relief as typhoon-prone regions lose emergency services
• Organizations must bring EU diplomats to meetings to demonstrate legitimacy to local officials
• Practice intensified under Duterte (2016-2022), continues under current President Marcos Jr

items.title: OONI Advances Probe Security with Anonymous Digital Transaction Framework
items.summary: OONI published research on enhancing probe security using principles from David Chaum's anonymous transaction systems, enabling censorship measurement without user identification.
items.content_text:
OONI released new research on probe security without identification requirements
• Work builds on David Chaum's foundational anonymous transaction system concepts
• Addresses critical need for privacy-preserving internet censorship measurement
• Enables users to contribute to censorship research without exposing their identity
• Research supported by Taiwan's Open Culture Foundation (OCF) community
• Post available in Mandarin, indicating focus on Chinese-speaking regions
• Framework could protect activists and researchers in high-surveillance environments

items.title: AI Tools Create New Censorship & Information Access Risks
