items.summary: AccessNow analysis shows LLMs pose availability risks through data deletion, service disruptions, and content refusals. DeepSeek censors Tiananmen Square topics, while open-source models enable bias removal.
items.content_text:
AccessNow examines how AI/LLMs impact information availability and system access
• German professor lost two years of data when ChatGPT deleted all chats after settings change
• AI agents can disrupt availability by wiping data or making unauthorized system changes
• Chinese DeepSeek R1 refuses to answer questions about Tiananmen Square and sensitive topics
• Perplexity AI successfully retrained open-source DeepSeek model to remove censorship
• Google Bard allegedly refused all questions containing Gaza/IDF keywords
• Closed-source models from Google/OpenAI lack transparency in their refusal mechanisms
• Open-source AI models enable customization and reduce dependency on corporate gatekeepers

items.title: OONI Documents Telegram Blocks During Kenya Exams, Supports Legal Challenge
items.summary: OONI provided technical evidence of Telegram blocking during Kenya's national exams in 2023 and 2024, which is being used in a High Court case challenging internet shutdowns.
items.content_text:
OONI documented systematic Telegram blocking during Kenya's KCSE national exams in November 2023 and November 2024
• Blocking targeted Telegram website, web interface, and app endpoints across multiple ISPs
• Technical measurements show coordinated censorship likely aimed at preventing exam cheating
• OONI's data is being used as evidence in High Court of Kenya petition filed in May 2025
• Case represents landmark public interest litigation challenging unlawful internet disruptions
• Demonstrates how technical measurement organizations can support legal resistance to censorship
• BAKE and 6 other organizations are challenging the shutdowns in court

items.title: Human Rights Groups Challenge Sandvine Rebrand After Entity List Removal
items.summary: Access Now demands AppLogic Networks (formerly Sandvine) prove its surveillance tech reforms after U.S. removed company from Entity List, citing lack of transparency on Egypt exit and failure to remedy DPI abuses.
items.content_text:
U.S. Commerce Dept removed AppLogic Networks (formerly Sandvine) from Entity List in October 2024 citing announced reforms
• Company was blacklisted in February 2024 for enabling Egyptian mass surveillance via deep packet inspection (DPI) technology
• DPI equipment was used for web monitoring, censorship, and spyware injection against dissidents and human rights defenders
• AppLogic committed to exiting Egypt by December 31, 2025 and consulting with civil society groups
• Access Now reports company has failed to engage with Egyptian civil society despite outreach attempts
• No transparent confirmation provided of complete Egypt exit or remediation plans for remaining equipment
• Human rights groups warn Entity List removal based on PR statements rather than verifiable actions
• Case highlights fragility of accountability mechanisms for surveillance tech companies

items.title: OONI Data Shows Iran Blocks Women's Rights Websites Since 2014
items.summary: OONI measurement data documents systematic blocking of women's rights websites in Iran since at least 2014, with censorship intensifying despite 2022 Women, Life, Freedom protests.
items.content_text:
OONI network measurements confirm Iran blocks women's rights websites since 2014
• Censorship has persisted despite widespread Women, Life, Freedom protests following Mahsa Jina Amini's death in 2022
• UN reports that repression of women in Iran has intensified in recent years
• OONI Explorer data provides technical evidence of systematic information control
• Women's rights websites join broader pattern of severely restricted information access in Iran
• Demonstrates need for censorship circumvention tools targeting gender-focused activism
• Technical measurement data offers verifiable documentation of state repression

items.title: OONI Study Reveals Inconsistent DNS Censorship Infrastructure Across French ISPs
items.summary: OONI analysis shows France implementing widespread website blocking via DNS manipulation across ISPs, but with inconsistent enforcement that creates escalation risks toward more invasive censorship methods.
items.content_text:
OONI researchers documented extensive DNS-based website blocking across France's four major commercial ISPs in fall 2024
• Censorship targets include copyright infringement, sports streaming, gambling, pornography, Russian organizations, and cybercrime sites
• Implementation is highly inconsistent between ISPs, with different providers blocking different subsets of court-ordered sites
• Blocking operates at DNS resolver level, making it easily bypassable with alternative DNS services
• French courts and administrative agencies have expanded blocking powers through multiple recent laws and court orders
• TikTok was blocked in New Caledonia during civil unrest, showing political application of censorship infrastructure
• Researchers warn that easily-bypassed current system may drive authorities toward more invasive deep packet inspection
• Study provides detailed legal framework analysis covering both judiciary and administrative censorship authorities

items.title: MTN Enabled Gov't Bounty SMS System Linked to Extrajudicial Killings in Congo
items.summary: Access Now demands MTN Group investigate how Congo's presidential security forces used MTN's network to send bounty SMS messages offering rewards for informants, amid reports of summary executions.
items.content_text:
Congo's DGSP (presidential security) sent SMS bounties via MTN Congo offering money for gang informants
• Campaign linked to crackdown beginning Sept 2024 that reportedly includes summary executions
• DGSP operates outside legal mandate - tasked with presidential security, not public order
• Access Now filed open letter demanding MTN Group investigate data breach and law enforcement practices
• Telco infrastructure weaponized for state surveillance enabling arbitrary detentions and disappearances
• MTN Group as majority shareholder of MTN Congo must review subscriber data sharing policies
• Case demonstrates how mobile networks can become surveillance tools for authoritarian governments
• Congo law specifically requires telcos to protect subscriber data - MTN allegedly violated duty of care

items.title: Reports: Binance Investigators Found Crypto Flows to Iran, Company Denies Firings
items.summary: Unverified reports claim Binance internal investigators discovered substantial crypto flows to Iran through user accounts, though Binance denies firing investigators over the findings.
items.content_text:
Unverified reports allege Binance investigators found significant crypto flows to Iran
• Flows reportedly occurred through regular Binance user accounts
• Binance has denied firing internal investigators related to these findings
• Iran faces extensive financial sanctions from US and other jurisdictions
• Crypto exchanges face regulatory pressure to enforce sanctions compliance
• Limited details available on scale, timeframe, or verification of alleged flows
• Story highlights ongoing tension between crypto accessibility and sanctions enforcement

items.title: 17 Nonprofits Challenge FTC's Retaliatory Investigation of Media Matters
items.summary: Coalition led by The Intercept's Press Freedom Defense Fund filed amicus brief to stop FTC's allegedly retaliatory investigation of Media Matters, citing pattern of government intimidation tactics.
items.content_text:
17 nonprofits filed amicus brief in U.S. Court of Appeals for D.C. Circuit
• Challenging FTC investigation into Media Matters for America over Trump administration criticism
• Brief argues investigation is retaliatory and part of broader government intimidation pattern
• Coalition includes EFF, Freedom of the Press Foundation, and other freedom tech organizations
• Seeks to uphold preliminary injunction against the investigation
• Brief cites similar tactics across Justice Department and FCC under current administration
• Legal strategy aims to prevent chilling effect on nonprofit journalism and research

items.title: Gabon Blocks Social Media Platforms During Anti-Government Protests
items.summary: Gabon's government indefinitely blocked Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube during ongoing protests, with 345+ rights organizations demanding immediate restoration of access.
items.content_text:
Jean-Claude Mendome of Gabon's High Authority for Communication announced indefinite social media shutdown on Feb 17, 2026
• OONI confirmed blocking of Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube as of Feb 18, 2026
• Shutdown coincides with anti-government protests and labor strikes across the country
• Government claims blocks prevent 'spread of false information' and protect national unity
• 345+ human rights organizations from #KeepItOn coalition demand immediate access restoration
• Gabon has pattern of shutdowns: 2023 elections, 2021 COVID protests, 2019 attempted coup, 2016 post-election
• Coalition calls on ISPs (Moov Gabon, Gabon Telecom, Airtel Gabon) to refuse government shutdown orders
• Violations cited include Gabonese Constitution Article 1(2) and international human rights frameworks

items.title: Bruce Schneier: AI Arms Races Threaten Democratic Participation and Concentrate Power in Big Tech
items.summary: Security expert Bruce Schneier warns that AI is creating adversarial arms races across domains like government-citizen interactions, benefiting Big Tech monopolies while potentially undermining authentic democratic participation.
items.content_text:
Schneier identifies AI-driven arms races across multiple domains: academia, legal systems, open source development, and democratic processes
• Government-citizen interactions increasingly involve AI on both sides - constituents using AI to generate comments, agencies using AI to process them
• 2017 FCC comment fraud exemplifies early version: broadband providers and activists both used bots to flood regulatory feedback
• One-fifth of Consumer Financial Protection Bureau submissions reportedly AI-generated by 2025
• US Congress staff using AI for constituent correspondence; politicians automating fundraising and outreach
• Primary beneficiaries are Big Tech corporations extracting trillions from AI infrastructure and model development
• Schneier advocates dual strategy: use AI for community advantage while resisting corporate power concentration
• Suggests antitrust regulation, human rights protections, and public AI alternatives as resistance tools

items.title: Microsoft Shares BitLocker Recovery Keys with FBI, Compromising Disk Encryption
items.summary: Microsoft routinely provides BitLocker backup keys to law enforcement like the FBI, undermining the privacy protection of Windows' built-in disk encryption for users storing keys in Microsoft accounts.
items.content_text:
Microsoft routinely hands over BitLocker recovery keys to law enforcement agencies including FBI [Forbes]
• BitLocker is Windows' built-in full-disk encryption, with Device Encryption variant on Windows Home
• Recovery keys automatically backed up to Microsoft accounts on Windows Home devices
• Windows Pro/Enterprise users can choose where to store recovery keys (USB, file, print, Active Directory)
• TPM 2.0 chip protects encryption keys on modern systems, but recovery keys remain accessible to Microsoft
• Device seizure while powered off provides strong protection unless recovery key is compromised
• Users relying on Microsoft account backup effectively give government access to encrypted data

items.title: Turkey Throttles Social Media During Political Protests - OONI Documents Technical Evidence
items.summary: OONI documented systematic throttling of major social media platforms and blocking of Telegram during March 2025 protests in Turkey following arrest of Istanbul mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu.
items.content_text:
Protests erupted across Turkey on March 19, 2025 following arrest of Istanbul mayor and presidential candidate Ekrem İmamoğlu
• OONI network measurements detected throttling of Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, YouTube, TikTok, and Signal
• Telegram access was completely blocked rather than throttled
• YouTube specifically blocked via DNS on TurkNet (AS12735) network
• Interference occurred across multiple ISPs during both March and September 2025 protest periods
• Technical evidence shows coordinated government response using different censorship techniques
• Pattern demonstrates sophisticated approach: throttling vs blocking based on platform

items.title: Dave Farber, 'Grandfather of Internet' and EFF Board Member, Dies at 91
items.summary: Dave Farber, longest-serving EFF Board member and internet pioneer known as the 'Grandfather of the Internet,' has died at 91, ending decades of advocacy for digital rights and open source protections.
items.content_text:
Dave Farber, 91, died in Tokyo where he was Distinguished Professor at Keio University
• Served as EFF's longest-tenured Board member since early 1990s, before WWW creation
• Known as 'Grandfather of the Internet' for foundational contributions to internet development
• Managed influential 'Interesting People' email list connecting internet pioneers globally
• Served as Chief Technologist at U.S. Federal Communications Commission (2000-2001)
• Mentored generations of internet developers and open source community members
• Consistently advocated for user rights and developer liberties throughout internet's growth
• Provided credibility and institutional knowledge to EFF's mission for over 30 years

items.title: Cryptographers Find Practical Attacks on Fiat-Shamir Zero-Knowledge Proofs
items.summary: New research reveals vulnerabilities in Fiat-Shamir heuristic used in ZK proof systems, showing how attackers can potentially prove false statements in widely-used cryptographic protocols.
items.content_text:
Paper by Khovratovich, Rothblum and Soukhanov demonstrates practical attacks on Fiat-Shamir heuristic
• Fiat-Shamir transforms interactive ZK proofs into non-interactive ones using hash functions
• Vulnerability allows proving false statements in systems that appear secure
• Attack exploits gap between theoretical security models and real-world implementations
• Affects verifiable computation systems commonly used in blockchain and privacy applications
• Research highlights ongoing tension between practical crypto deployment and theoretical guarantees
• Issue stems from replacing random oracles with actual hash functions in practice

items.title: Suriname Blocks Meta Platforms During Cost of Living Protests
items.summary: Suriname restricted Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger on government network Telesur during February 2023 protests when demonstrators stormed parliament over economic crisis.
items.content_text:
NetBlocks confirmed restriction of Meta platforms (Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, Messenger) in Suriname February 17-18, 2023
• Blocks implemented on government-owned telecom network Telesur (AS27775) during cost of living protests
• Protesters stormed National Assembly in Paramaribo amid economic crisis and currency devaluation
• Social media restrictions lasted from Friday evening through Saturday morning
• NetBlocks verified disruption using real-time metrics from 100 measurement points across 10 vantage points
• VPN services confirmed as effective workaround for circumventing restrictions
• Exceptional measure for Suriname, which lacks significant internet censorship history
• Government condemned protests and established task force to manage situation

items.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalist Equipment, Target Sources in Deportation Investigation
items.summary: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists covering secret US migrant deportations, seized their devices, and threatened to access confidential sources by circumventing passwords.
items.content_text:
Four journalists detained 5 hours by Cameroon judicial police while covering secret US deportations
• Police seized phones, laptop, and other equipment from AP reporters and freelancer
• Authorities investigating for criminal trespass, equipment not yet returned
• Police reportedly plan to circumvent device passwords to access private information
• Lawyer concerned anonymous sources could be compromised through device forensics
• Journalists threatened with being 'crushed' and told one would 'disappear without a trace'
• Incident highlights operational security risks for journalists in hostile environments
• Demonstrates how device seizures can expose source networks and communications

items.title: DHS Using Administrative Subpoenas to Demand User Data from Tech Companies
items.summary: Department of Homeland Security is abusing administrative subpoenas to force tech companies like Google to hand over names and locations of users who criticize government online.
items.content_text:
DHS demanding user names, locations, and personal data from tech companies using administrative subpoenas
• Targeting users who criticize government online according to EFF reporting
• Administrative subpoenas bypass normal court oversight processes unlike traditional warrants
• EFF providing guidance to tech companies on how to resist these requests
• Part of broader pattern including ICE facial recognition surveillance programs
• EFF campaigning for expanded end-to-end encryption protections in response
• Section 230 protections identified as critical defense for online speech rights

items.title: Gabon Shuts Down Social Media Amid Protests, Coalition Demands Restoration
items.summary: Gabon's government ordered indefinite social media shutdown during anti-government protests, blocking Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram and YouTube. 345+ human rights orgs demand immediate restoration.
items.content_text:
High Authority for Communication ordered indefinite social media suspension on February 17, 2026
• OONI confirms blocking of Facebook, WhatsApp, TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube as of February 18
• Shutdown coincides with ongoing anti-government protests and labor strikes
• #KeepItOn coalition of 345+ organizations from 105 countries demands immediate reversal
• Government claims measures prevent 'false information' spread and protect national unity
• Follows pattern: shutdowns in 2023 elections, 2021 COVID protests, 2019 coup attempt, 2016 election protests
• Coalition calls on ISPs (Moov Gabon, Gabon Telecom, Airtel Gabon) to resist shutdown orders
• Evidence shows shutdowns actually amplify misinformation rather than reduce it

items.title: Angola's Parliament Passes Restrictive NGO Bill Threatening Civil Society Operations
items.summary: Angola's parliament approved legislation granting sweeping government control over NGOs, including mandatory authorization, extensive monitoring, financial restrictions, and administrative suspension powers that undermine civil society independence.
items.content_text:
Angola's parliament passed restrictive NGO regulation bill on January 22, 2026 with 106 votes in favor
• Bill transforms freedom of association from right to government-granted privilege requiring administrative authorization
• Authorities gain power to monitor activities/finances, determine project locations, and assess leadership 'suitability'
• Administrative suspension allowed up to 120 days based on vague 'strong indications' of unlawful conduct
• Financial controls mandate all funds through domestic banks and ban international capital transfers
• Vague terminology like 'immoral' and 'non-compliant' grants wide discretionary power to authorities
• UNITA opposition party voted against, criticizing shift of control from courts to administrative bodies
• Local civil society groups warn law is unconstitutional and will further shrink civic space
• Parliament also approved anti-misinformation bill, signaling broader crackdown on civic freedoms

items.title: Guinea Blocks Social Media During Protests, Army Called In
items.summary: Guinea restricted Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, TikTok and other platforms on May 17, 2023 as authorities deployed army to suppress planned protests.
items.content_text:
NetBlocks confirmed social media restrictions across Guinea on May 17, 2023
• Facebook, WhatsApp, Instagram, TikTok blocked on Orange and MTN networks
• Restrictions coincide with government calling in army to assist police against protests
• Measurements taken from 860 data points across 18 vantage points in Guinea
• NetBlocks notes VPN services can circumvent these government censorship measures
• Government cites maintaining public order as justification for restrictions
• Pattern follows previous internet shutdowns during Guinea's elections and political events
• Disruptions impact fundamental rights including freedom of expression and assembly

items.title: NetBlocks Documents Systematic Internet Shutdowns During School Exams Across MENA
items.summary: NetBlocks documented coordinated internet shutdowns and platform restrictions across Kenya, Iraq, Syria, and Algeria during 2023 school exams, revealing systematic government censorship tactics.
items.content_text:
Kenya selectively blocked Telegram during morning exam hours in November 2023, targeting exam paper leak channels
• Iraq implemented 4-hour daily internet shutdowns (4-8 AM) during June and August exam periods, excluding Kurdistan region
• Syria conducted 4-hour morning internet blackouts in May, June, and August coinciding with exam schedules
• Algeria throttled internet connectivity twice daily matching morning/afternoon exam times during June Baccalaureate exams
• Kurdistan region operated independently with 2-hour shutdowns (6-8 AM) during high school exams
• Kenya authorities arrested 6 Telegram channel administrators running exam leak groups with 18,000+ members
• Shutdowns demonstrate government capability for rapid, coordinated digital infrastructure control
• Pattern reveals systematic use of internet disruption as policy tool across multiple authoritarian contexts

items.title: Wikipedia Defends Volunteer Editors Against Rising Takedown Demands
items.summary: Wikimedia Foundation received 664 takedown requests in 2024 (up from 304 over two years a decade ago) but granted only 4, defending community-governed content creation through Section 230 protections.
items.content_text:
Takedown requests against Wikipedia content jumped from 304 over two years (decade ago) to 664 in 2024 alone
• Wikimedia Foundation granted only 4 of the 664 requests, defending volunteer editors through expanded legal team
• Section 230 enables community dispute resolution rather than centralized content moderation
• Without Section 230, potential litigation costs could bankrupt the platform (~$20k minimum per court filing)
• EU lacks Section 230 protections; Digital Services Act allows courts to order content removal via injunctions
• German court rejected Wikipedia sources as insufficient, demanding state media over trade publications
• EU litigation costs range from $30k per hearing to hundreds of thousands for complex cases
• Pseudonymous editing protection critical for sensitive topics like political history and religious content

items.title: Access Now Report: AI Tools Systematically Undermine Data Confidentiality and Privacy
items.summary: Major AI platforms like ChatGPT and Meta AI are compromising user privacy through data breaches, lack of encryption, and forced integration that breaks E2EE promises.
items.content_text:
Access Now published comprehensive analysis of AI security vulnerabilities threatening confidentiality
• DeepSeek exposed publicly accessible database with chat histories and sensitive user data [Wiz Research]
• OpenAI suffered breach exposing names, emails, location data via third-party analytics provider
• Meta AI integration in WhatsApp breaks E2EE by default - summaries pass through Meta servers unencrypted
• Urban Cyber Security VPN extensions promising 'AI protection' actually harvested 8 million users' LLM data
• New York Times lawsuit seeks access to 20+ million private ChatGPT conversations for copyright case
• Most AI chatbots lack end-to-end encryption as standard feature, leaving chat histories exposed
• Emerging 'agentic AI' with system-level access creates new attack vectors via prompt injection
• Report frames issues using CIA triad (Confidentiality-Integrity-Availability) security model

items.title: Cameroon Police Seize Journalists' Equipment Investigating US Migrant Deportations
items.summary: Cameroonian police detained 4 journalists for 5 hours, seized their phones/laptops while investigating secret US deportation flights, threatening to breach device security and expose confidential sources.
items.content_text:
Four journalists detained by Cameroon judicial police while covering secret US migrant deportations
• AP reporters Nalova Akua, Angel Ngwe, Arnold Ndal plus freelancer Randy Joe Sa'ah held for 5 hours
• Police confiscated cellphones, laptop, and other equipment under criminal trespass investigation
• Officers threatened to circumvent device passwords and access private information
• Lawyer warned anonymous sources could be compromised through device forensics
• Journalists threatened with being 'crushed' and told one would 'disappear without a trace'
• Equipment remains with police despite no formal charges filed against journalists
• Incident highlights digital security risks for journalists in authoritarian environments

items.title: Iraq Blocks Telegram Nationwide, Kurdistan Region Remains Online
items.summary: Iraq restricted Telegram across most of the country citing national security and leaked personal data concerns, while the autonomous Kurdistan region maintains access.
items.content_text:
Iraq's Ministry of Communications ordered Telegram suspension on August 6, 2023 over national security and personal data concerns
• NetBlocks confirmed restriction affects major ISPs Zain and Earthlink but not Kurdish operators Kurdistan Net and Korek Telecom
• Block excludes autonomous Iraqi Kurdistan region, creating geographic disparity in access
• Restriction targets both Telegram frontends and backends, indicating comprehensive technical implementation
• VPN services can circumvent the censorship measures according to NetBlocks analysis
• Follows pattern of Iraq using internet restrictions during political crises and exam periods
• Telegram's popularity with activists and relatively unregulated status makes it frequent government target

items.title: Microsoft Routinely Shares BitLocker Recovery Keys with FBI
items.summary: Proton explains BitLocker encryption following revelations that Microsoft regularly provides BitLocker recovery keys to law enforcement agencies like the FBI.
items.content_text:
Microsoft routinely hands over BitLocker backup keys to law enforcement agencies including FBI [Forbes]
• BitLocker is Windows' built-in full-disk encryption, with Device Encryption as simplified version on Windows Home
• Device Encryption automatically backs up recovery keys to Microsoft accounts by default
• Windows Pro/Enterprise BitLocker allows users to choose where recovery keys are stored (USB, file, printed copy)
• TPM 2.0 chip provides hardware-based protection but keys still recoverable via Microsoft accounts
• Protection strong against theft/loss when powered off, but vulnerable to legal process targeting Microsoft
• Demonstrates risks of relying on proprietary encryption with centralized key backup systems

items.title: EFF Report: How 'Free' Surveillance Tech Creates Accountability Gaps and Mass Data Sharing
items.summary: EFF analysis reveals how surveillance vendors use 'free' trials, continued service without funding, and donations to bypass local oversight, creating data pipelines to ICE and eroding civil liberties.
items.content_text:
Surveillance vendors offer 'free' ALPRs, face recognition, drones, and data platforms to bypass local oversight processes
• Denver running competing drone trials from Flock Safety and Skydio/Axon for drone-as-first-responder programs through August 2026
• Companies continue providing services even after funding ends - Fall River used ShotSpotter for free after refusing $90k annual cost
• Denver mayor kept Flock ALPR cameras running despite city council unanimously rejecting $666k contract extension
• Data sharing agreements create pipelines to ICE, undermining sanctuary city protections
• Subscription models mean 'free' tools eventually require taxpayer funding for expanded feature sets
• Community groups launched grassroots campaigns demanding surveillance systems be shut off, not just defunded
• EFF recommends cities reject federal grants, vendor trials, and donations that facilitate surveillance experimentation

items.title: Citizen Lab Submits UN Reports on Surveillance Tech Enabling Enforced Disappearances
items.summary: Citizen Lab researchers submitted two reports to UN bodies detailing how targeted surveillance technology facilitates enforced disappearances and transnational repression of migrants.
items.content_text:
Citizen Lab co-authored two submissions to UN Committee on Enforced Disappearances
• First submission focuses on digital tools enabling state-sponsored disappearances
• Report calls on host states to protect against digital transnational repression
• Second submission examines how migration processes increase vulnerability to repression
• Authors highlight risks from inadequate privacy safeguards and data sharing agreements
• Both reports emphasize host state responsibility to protect vulnerable populations
• Submissions provide policy recommendations for addressing surveillance-enabled rights violations

items.title: Ethiopia Revokes Independent Media License in Escalating Press Crackdown
items.summary: Ethiopian authorities revoked Addis Standard's media license citing vague 'national interest' violations, part of broader crackdown including Reuters accreditation denials and newsroom raids.
items.content_text:
Ethiopian Media Authority (EMA) revoked Addis Standard's online media registration on February 24, 2026
• EMA cited 'media ethics violations' and threats to 'national interests' without providing specific evidence
• Editor-in-Chief Yonas Kedir denies receiving formal violation notices, calls claims 'factually incorrect'
• Part of broader media suppression: Reuters journalists denied accreditation, AFP reporter blocked from Tigray travel
• Addis Standard previously raided in April 2025 with equipment seized and managers detained
• Creates immediate need for censorship-resistant publishing and secure communication channels
• Pattern indicates systematic dismantling of independent journalism infrastructure in Ethiopia

items.title: Coalition Demands California Revoke Federal License Plate Reader Permits
items.summary: EFF and 25+ organizations demand California revoke permits allowing CBP and DEA to deploy automated license plate readers disguised as traffic barrels along border highways.
items.content_text:
Coalition led by EFF and Imperial Valley Equity & Justice sent letter to Gov. Newsom and Caltrans Director
• Demands revocation of permits for federal ALPR surveillance on California highways
• CBP, DEA, and Border Patrol using ALPRs disguised as traffic barrels for mass surveillance
• Coalition cites risks of harassment, detention, and deportation for residents and migrants
• 25+ organizations signed letter including ACLU California Action, Fight for the Future, EPIC
• Timing linked to return of USBP Chief Greg Bovino to El Centro sector
• State-level pushback against federal surveillance infrastructure deployment

items.title: Pakistan Jails Human Rights Lawyers 17 Years for Military Criticism Tweets
items.summary: Two Pakistani human rights lawyers sentenced to 17 years under cybercrime law PECA for tweets criticizing military abuses in Balochistan, demonstrating weaponization of digital legislation against dissent.
items.content_text:
Imaan Mazari-Hazir and Hadi Ali Chattha sentenced January 24, 2026 for 2021-2025 tweets about military abuses
• Charged under Pakistan's Prevention of Electronic Crimes Act (PECA) Sections 9, 10, 11, and 26A
• Trial moved unusually fast - indictment to sentencing in under 3 months vs typical years-long timeline
• Evidence consisted of tweet screenshots and witness statements; defense denied adequate cross-examination
• Couple represented Baloch activists, missing persons families, and journalists facing state harassment
• PECA originally designed for cybercrime now systematically used to silence critics of state institutions
• International condemnation from UN Human Rights Office, EU, Amnesty International as judicial persecution
• Part of broader pattern - at least 23 documented cases of journalists targeted under PECA since 2021

items.title: Uganda Implements Election Internet Shutdown, Threatens VPN Users with Device Targeting
items.summary: Uganda shut down internet and blocked major platforms during 2026 elections despite international pressure. Authorities warn VPN users could be targeted via their devices, highlighting escalating digital surveillance threats.
items.content_text:
Uganda Communications Commission ordered 95% internet shutdown during January 2026 elections [Cloudflare Radar/IODA data]
• WhatsApp, Instagram, X, Facebook, and mobile money services remain blocked even after partial restoration
• UCC explicitly threatened VPN users could be 'targeted via their devices' for bypassing restrictions
• Starlink services were disabled weeks before elections, cutting satellite internet backup
• Pattern continues from 2016 and 2021 elections when Uganda imposed similar shutdowns and VPN blocks
• Access Now and #KeepItOn coalition documented real-time shutdown despite prior government commitments
• African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights issued statement condemning the restrictions
• Demonstrates urgent need for censorship-resistant infrastructure in authoritarian contexts

items.title: OONI and Partners Host Internet Measurement Village at Global Gathering 2025
items.summary: Four major internet measurement projects are hosting an 'OMG Village' at Global Gathering 2025 in Portugal to advance censorship monitoring and shutdown detection capabilities.
items.content_text:
OONI hosting Open Measurement Gathering (OMG) Village at Global Gathering 2025 in Estoril, Portugal
• Four core partners: OONI, Measurement Lab (M-Lab), Internet Outage Detection & Analysis (IODA), and Censored Planet
• Additional participants include Netalitica and Project Ainita hosting booths
• Focus on internet shutdown investigation, censorship detection tools, and open datasets
• Community space for researchers, activists, and builders working on network interference
• Collaboration between established measurement platforms strengthens censorship detection ecosystem
• Event provides opportunity for knowledge transfer on technical measurement methodologies

items.title: Ukraine: 10 Investigative Journalists Surveilled by Criminal Network Tied to Government Officials
items.summary: Ukrainian authorities are investigating surveillance of 10 corruption journalists by a criminal network including government officials, discovered during a 15-month anti-corruption probe called 'Operation Midas'.
items.content_text:
National Anti-Corruption Bureau (NABU) uncovered surveillance of 10 Ukrainian journalists covering corruption during 'Operation Midas' investigation
• Criminal network allegedly included government officials and businessmen who embezzled energy sector funds
• Journalists' personal information found on seized digital devices alongside data on politicians and security officials
• Committee to Protect Journalists calls for swift investigation into privacy violations
• Nine journalist names publicly released, including editors from Forbes Ukraine, Censor.NET, and investigative outlet Nashi Groshi
• Two of the surveilled journalists are now deceased
• Pattern follows January 2024 case where 30 SBU officers spied on Bihus.Info journalists
• Highlights operational security vulnerabilities for investigative journalists in conflict zones

items.title: Signal Discusses Global Tech Industry US-China Centralization
items.summary: Signal's official blog examines how the tech industry is centered in the US and China due to historical material conditions rather than superior talent, touching on implications for global tech development.
items.content_text:
Signal published blog post analyzing tech industry geographic concentration
• Industry centered in US and China due to historical material conditions, not superior talent
• US advantages traced to early military investments in networked computation
• Timeline includes US becoming global superpower, privatization/commercialization of networks, dot-com boom
• Post appears to address Signal's global accessibility and mission
• Analysis provides context for why freedom tech tools often emerge from specific regions
• Suggests broader discussion about democratizing secure communications globally

items.title: Ethiopia Shutters Independent Media Outlets Ahead of National Elections
items.summary: Ethiopian authorities revoked the license of Addis Standard, one of the country's last independent news outlets, three months before national elections, escalating systematic media suppression.
items.content_text:
Ethiopia's Media Authority revoked operating license of Addis Standard on February 24, 2026
• Action taken 3 months before scheduled national elections
• Addis Standard was one of Ethiopia's last remaining independent media outlets since 2011
• Government cited unspecified violations of laws and media ethics, endangering national interests
• Editor-in-chief Yonas Kedir denied allegations, said no formal prior violations were communicated
• Part of escalating crackdown: Deutsche Welle journalists suspended, Wazema Radio license revoked, Reuters accreditation declined
• Pattern follows government raids, detentions, and harassment of independent journalists throughout 2025-2026
• Demonstrates urgent need for censorship-resistant communication infrastructure in authoritarian contexts

items.title: Coalition Demands California Revoke Federal ALPR Surveillance Permits on Border Highways
items.summary: EFF-led coalition demands California revoke permits allowing CBP and DEA to deploy automated license plate readers disguised as traffic barrels on border highways, citing mass surveillance risks.
items.content_text:
Coalition of 25+ orgs led by EFF and Imperial Valley Equity & Justice sent letter to Gov. Newsom and Caltrans
• Federal agencies CBP and DEA have permits to deploy ALPRs along California border highways
• Surveillance devices often disguised as traffic barrels to avoid detection
• Coalition argues ALPRs put residents and migrants at risk of harassment, detention, and deportation
• Letter references return of USBP Chief Greg Bovino who led 'brutal campaigns' in other cities
• Demands immediate revocation of permits and removal of existing surveillance infrastructure
• Broad coalition includes nurses, teachers, ACLU, privacy groups, and immigrant rights organizations

items.title: Signal Deploys Faster ORAM for Privacy-Preserving Contact Discovery
items.summary: Signal improved their contact discovery system using advanced ORAM techniques and enclaves, laying groundwork for usernames and enhanced phone number privacy controls.
items.content_text:
Signal enhanced Contact Discovery Service Infrastructure (CDSI) with new ORAM implementation
• System lets users find Signal contacts without revealing social graphs to Signal servers
• Uses secure enclaves and oblivious memory access patterns to protect privacy
• New implementation is faster and more efficient than previous version
• Technical foundation enables upcoming username feature and phone number privacy controls
• Represents advancement in privacy-preserving identity infrastructure
• Demonstrates scalable approach to private contact discovery for secure messaging

items.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Webinar Series Targeting Civil Society Spear-Phishing Threats
items.summary: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline launches webinar series starting March 2026, focusing on spear-phishing defense for activists and civil society with expert speakers from Citizen Lab and security industry.
items.content_text:
Access Now announces 'Safe and Strong' webinar series on digital security starting March 10, 2026
• First session focuses on spear-phishing attacks targeting activists, journalists, and civil society
• Expert speakers include Citizen Lab's Rebekah Brown and threat researchers from Lookout
• Training draws from documented attack cases in Eastern Europe and Middle East regions
• Series aims to share actionable threat prevention and response insights
• Initiative brings together civil society, private sector, and support organizations
• Content specifically addresses increasingly sophisticated social engineering techniques

items.title: Iran's Internet Blackout Enabled Mass State Violence and Narrative Control
items.summary: Iran's 15-day internet shutdown during 2026 protests enabled unprecedented state violence (reportedly 30,000+ deaths) while regime-aligned diaspora voices filled the information vacuum to control international narratives.
items.content_text:
Iran imposed total internet shutdown Jan 8-23, 2026 during nationwide protests across 30+ provinces
• NetBlocks confirmed near-complete connectivity collapse affecting mobile, fixed-line, and international gateways
• UN Human Rights Council described resulting state violence as 'unprecedented in scope and brutality'
• Anonymous state officials reportedly confirm over 30,000 deaths; HRANA documents 41,800-50,000 arrests
• Regime-aligned diaspora voices like NIAC's Trita Parsi filled information vacuum during blackout
• Iranian state media discussed building 'Iran lobby' in US, explicitly naming Parsi and NIAC as assets
• Systematic disinformation playbook deployed: questioning evidence, demanding impossible proof standards
• Pattern mirrors 2019 shutdown and Woman Life Freedom movement information control tactics

items.title: Discord Rolls Out Mandatory Age Verification Despite Data Breach History
items.summary: Discord begins global mandatory age verification requiring government ID or face scans for many users, despite winning EFF's 2025 'Breachie Award' for exposing 70k users' identity documents in a hack.
items.content_text:
Discord launching mandatory age verification globally starting March 2026, not required by law in most jurisdictions
• Users estimated under 18 or with insufficient data face restrictions unless they upload government ID or face scan
• Third-party vendors k-ID (global) and Persona (UK) now handle verification after 2025 breach exposed 70,000 users' documents
• EFF warns this creates surveillance infrastructure and chills anonymous speech for vulnerable groups
• Users locked into 'teen experience' face content filters, DM restrictions, and blocked community features
• Age inference system uses account tenure, device data, and platform patterns to automatically sort most adults
• Facial age estimation technology is 'notoriously unreliable' especially for marginalized groups [EFF]
• Platform promises to delete IDs and keep face scans on-device, but users have no independent verification

items.title: Access Now Launches Digital Security Training Series for Civil Society
items.summary: Access Now's Digital Security Helpline is hosting webinar series starting March 2026, focusing on spear-phishing defense for activists, journalists, and civil society facing targeted attacks.
items.content_text:
Access Now launching 'Safe and Strong' webinar series on digital security for civil society
• First session March 10, 2026 focuses on spear-phishing attacks targeting activists and journalists
• Training draws from documented attack cases in Eastern Europe and Middle East regions
• Expert speakers include Citizen Lab researcher, Lookout threat analyst, and regional security practitioners
• Series addresses increasing complexity of digital attacks against civil society organizations
• Content specifically tailored for high-risk users including human rights defenders
• Training includes practical examples and best practices for prevention and incident response

items.title: Senegal Arrests Second Journalist for Challenging Official Account of Student Death
items.summary: Senegalese authorities arrested freelance commentator Abdou Nguer for questioning prosecutor's denial of torture in student death during university protest crackdown, marking escalating press freedom concerns.
items.content_text:
Abdou Nguer arrested February 19, 2026 for challenging prosecutor's statement on student death cause
• Charged with spreading false news for disputing official dismissal of torture allegations
• Second journalist detained over university protest coverage after Sen TV's Modou Fall (arrested Feb 11)
• Nguer questioned prosecutor Ousmane Ndoye's claim student fell from building vs medical autopsy showing traumas
• Student died after police stormed university campus in Dakar during protests
• Nguer previously detained 7 months in 2025, served 3 months for 'offending head of state'
• Pattern of false news charges targeting journalists covering state violence and public affairs
• Committee to Protect Journalists calls systematic intimidation of Senegalese press
